1Ôćĺpackage cmd
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺimport (
     4Ôćĺ	"fmt"
     5Ôćĺ	"strconv"
     6Ôćĺ	"time"
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
     9Ôćĺ	"github.com/usenurge/nurgesh/internal/account"
    10Ôćĺ	"github.com/usenurge/nurgesh/internal/db"
    11Ôćĺ	"github.com/usenurge/nurgesh/internal/email"
    12Ôćĺ)
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺvar syncCmd = &cobra.Command{
    15Ôćĺ	Use:   "sync",
    16Ôćĺ	Short: "Sync emails from connected sources",
    17Ôćĺ	Long:  "Fetches sent emails via Convex (last 90 days on first sync, incremental after).",
    18Ôćĺ	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    19Ôćĺ		d, err := db.Open()
    20Ôćĺ		if err != nil {
    21Ôćĺ			return fmt.Errorf("opening database: %w", err)
    22Ôćĺ		}
    23Ôćĺ		defer d.Close()
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ		af, err := account.LoadAuth()
    26Ôćĺ		if err != nil || af.Token == "" {
    27Ôćĺ			return fmt.Errorf("not logged in. Run: nurge account login")
    28Ôćĺ		}
    29Ôćĺ
    30Ôćĺ		client := account.NewConvexClient(GetConvexURL())
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ		// Read last sync version (stored as float string in a dedicated connector row)
    33Ôćĺ		var lastVersionStr string
    34Ôćĺ		d.QueryRow("SELECT COALESCE(sync_cursor, '0') FROM connectors WHERE id = 'gmail-convex'").Scan(&lastVersionStr)
    35Ôćĺ		lastVersion, _ := strconv.ParseFloat(lastVersionStr, 64)
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ		fmt.Println("  Syncing sent emails...")
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ		resp, err := client.SyncEmails(af.Token, lastVersion)
    40Ôćĺ		if err != nil {
    41Ôćĺ			return fmt.Errorf("sync failed: %w", err)
    42Ôćĺ		}
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ		if resp.Count == 0 {
    45Ôćĺ			fmt.Println("  Already up to date")
    46Ôćĺ			return nil
    47Ôćĺ		}
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ		// Convert to RawMessage
    50Ôćĺ		raws := make([]email.RawMessage, len(resp.Emails))
    51Ôćĺ		var maxVersion float64
    52Ôćĺ		for i, e := range resp.Emails {
    53Ôćĺ			raws[i] = email.RawMessage{
    54Ôćĺ				ID:        e.ExternalID,
    55Ôćĺ				Source:    e.Source,
    56Ôćĺ				Direction: e.Direction,
    57Ôćĺ				ThreadID:  e.ThreadID,
    58Ôćĺ				Subject:   e.Subject,
    59Ôćĺ				FromEmail: e.FromEmail,
    60Ôćĺ				ToEmails:  e.ToEmails,
    61Ôćĺ				CcEmails:  e.CcEmails,
    62Ôćĺ				Date:      e.Date,
    63Ôćĺ				Snippet:   e.Snippet,
    64Ôćĺ			}
    65Ôćĺ			if e.SyncVersion > maxVersion {
    66Ôćĺ				maxVersion = e.SyncVersion
    67Ôćĺ			}
    68Ôćĺ		}
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ		result, err := email.StoreConvexEmails(d, raws)
    71Ôćĺ		if err != nil {
    72Ôćĺ			return fmt.Errorf("storing emails: %w", err)
    73Ôćĺ		}
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ		// Store the max sync version for next incremental sync
    76Ôćĺ		d.Exec(
    77Ôćĺ			`INSERT INTO connectors (id, type, sync_cursor, last_sync_at, total_synced)
    78Ôćĺ			 VALUES ('gmail-convex', 'email', ?, datetime('now'), ?)
    79Ôćĺ			 ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET sync_cursor = ?, last_sync_at = datetime('now'), total_synced = total_synced + ?`,
    80Ôćĺ			strconv.FormatFloat(maxVersion, 'f', -1, 64), result.Stored,
    81Ôćĺ			strconv.FormatFloat(maxVersion, 'f', -1, 64), result.Stored,
    82Ôćĺ		)
    83Ôćĺ
    84Ôćĺ		fmt.Printf("  \033[32mÔťô\033[0m Synced %d emails (%d fetched) in %s\n",
    85Ôćĺ			result.Stored, result.Fetched, result.Duration.Round(time.Second))
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ		return nil
    88Ôćĺ	},
    89Ôćĺ}
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺfunc init() {
    92Ôćĺ	rootCmd.AddCommand(syncCmd)
    93Ôćĺ}
    94Ôćĺ

    10Ôćĺ

    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺconst (
    15Ôćĺ	loginTimeout = 120 * time.Second
    16Ôćĺ)
    17Ôćĺ
    18Ôćĺ// EnsureAuth checks for a valid auth token. If not found,
    19Ôćĺ// runs the interactive browser login flow.
    20Ôćĺ// Trusts the local token if it exists ÔÇö only re-auths when no token is present
    21Ôćĺ// or when an API call rejects it downstream.
    22Ôćĺ// All status messages are printed to stderr so stdout stays clean for --format json.
    23Ôćĺfunc EnsureAuth(convexURL, dashboardURL string) (*User, error) {
    24Ôćĺ	af, err := LoadAuth()
    25Ôćĺ	if err == nil && af.Token != "" {
    26Ôćĺ		return &User{Email: af.Email, Name: af.Name}, nil
    27Ôćĺ	}
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ	// Has refresh token but no nurge token? Try silent re-auth.
    30Ôćĺ	if err == nil && af.GoogleRefreshToken != "" {
    31Ôćĺ		user, silentErr := silentReauth(convexURL, af)
    32Ôćĺ		if silentErr == nil {
    33Ôćĺ			return user, nil
    34Ôćĺ		}
    35Ôćĺ		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  Silent re-auth failed, opening browser...\n")
    36Ôćĺ	}
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ	// No valid token ÔÇö start login flow
    39Ôćĺ	return Login(convexURL, dashboardURL)
    40Ôćĺ}
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ// SilentReauth attempts to get a fresh Nurge token using the stored Google
    43Ôćĺ// refresh token, without opening a browser. Called when a downstream API
    44Ôćĺ// call rejects the current token.
    45Ôćĺfunc SilentReauth(convexURL string) (*User, error) {
    46Ôćĺ	af, err := LoadAuth()
    47Ôćĺ	if err != nil || af.GoogleRefreshToken == "" {
    48Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no refresh token available")
    49Ôćĺ	}
    50Ôćĺ	return silentReauth(convexURL, af)
    51Ôćĺ}
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺfunc silentReauth(convexURL string, af *AuthFile) (*User, error) {
    54Ôćĺ	ctx := context.Background()
    55Ôćĺ
    56Ôćĺ	// Refresh the Google ID token
    57Ôćĺ	idToken, err := RefreshIDToken(ctx, af.GoogleRefreshToken)
    58Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
    59Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("refreshing google token: %w", err)
    60Ôćĺ	}
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ	// Exchange with Convex
    63Ôćĺ	client := NewConvexClient(convexURL)
    64Ôćĺ	resp, err := client.ExchangeGoogleToken(idToken)
    65Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
    66Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("exchanging google token: %w", err)
    67Ôćĺ	}
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ	// Update auth file with new nurge token
    70Ôćĺ	now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
    71Ôćĺ	af.Token = resp.Token
    72Ôćĺ	af.Email = resp.Email
    73Ôćĺ	af.Name = resp.Name
    74Ôćĺ	af.ValidatedAt = now
    75Ôćĺ	if err := SaveAuth(af); err != nil {
    76Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("saving auth: %w", err)
    77Ôćĺ	}
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ	return &User{Email: resp.Email, Name: resp.Name}, nil
    80Ôćĺ}
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ// Login runs the interactive browser-based Google OAuth login flow.
    83Ôćĺfunc Login(convexURL, dashboardURL string) (*User, error) {
    84Ôćĺ	// Generate state parameter
    85Ôćĺ	state := generateState()
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ	// Start local callback server (binds before opening browser)
    88Ôćĺ	port, resultCh, errCh, cleanup, err := util.ListenAndWait(loginTimeout)
    89Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
    90Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("starting callback server: %w", err)
    91Ôćĺ	}
    92Ôćĺ	defer cleanup()
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ	// Get Google OAuth consent URL
    95Ôćĺ	authURL, err := GetGoogleAuthURL(port, state)
    96Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
    97Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("building auth URL: %w", err)
    98Ôćĺ	}
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  Opening browser to log in...\n")

    26Ôćĺ		}
    27Ôćĺ
    28Ôćĺ		// Remove token file
    29Ôćĺ		tokenDir := fmt.Sprintf(".nurge/connectors/%s", id)
    30Ôćĺ		os.RemoveAll(tokenDir)
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ		// Remove from config
    33Ôćĺ		if err := project.RemoveConnector(id); err != nil {
    34Ôćĺ			return fmt.Errorf("updating config: %w", err)
    35Ôćĺ		}
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ		fmt.Printf("\033[32mÔťô\033[0m %s disconnected\n", id)
    38Ôćĺ		return nil
    39Ôćĺ	},
    40Ôćĺ}
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺfunc init() {
    43Ôćĺ	rootCmd.AddCommand(disconnectCmd)
    44Ôćĺ}

Perfect! Now I have enough information to write a comprehensive summary. Let me create the complete user flow document:

## NURGE COMPLETE USER FLOW - CODE ANALYSIS

Based on thorough exploration of the codebase, here is the complete user flow from actual implementation:

---

### **1. ENTRY POINT & ROOT COMMAND FLOW**

**File: `/cmd/root.go` and `main.go`**

**Entry:** `main()` Ôćĺ Sets build info Ôćĺ Calls `cmd.Execute()`

**Root Command Behavior:**
- `nurge [directory]` - shorthand for `nurge init [directory]`
- If first arg is not a known command/flag, rewrites `os.Args` to inject `init` 
- Pre-run hooks check for auth (skipped for `init`, `account login/logout`, `help`, `version`)
- Metrics logged for every command (except `init`, `metrics`, `version`)
- Update check runs async (2s timeout) and shows notification on stderr

---

### **2. INITIALIZATION FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/init.go` and `internal/project/init.go`, `internal/project/step_*.go`**

When user runs `nurge <directory>` or `nurge init [directory]`:

1. **Directory Setup** (runInit in root.go)
   - Creates directory if provided, changes to it
   - Validates directory is empty (unless `--force`)
   
2. **Project Initialization Steps** (Run() in init.go) - sequential execution:
   - **projectStep**: Creates `.nurge/` directory, initializes `config.json`
   - **databaseStep**: Creates SQLite database at `.nurge/nurge.db` with schema
   - **gitignoreStep**: Writes `.gitignore` to exclude `.nurge/`
   - **hooksStep** (optional, `--skip-hooks`): Installs Claude Code hooks
   - **skillStep**: Generates initial global setup skill in `.claude/skills/`
   - **gitStep**: Initializes git repository
   - **nextStepStep**: Prints next steps to user

**Config Structure** (`config.json`):
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "created_at": "RFC3339_timestamp",
  "connectors": { "id": { "connected": bool, "account": "email@domain" } },
  "setup_completed": false,
  "settings": {}
}
```

**Database**: `.nurge/nurge.db` (SQLite) - stores all project state

---

### **3. AUTH/ACCOUNT FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/account.go`, `internal/account/auth.go`, `internal/account/convex.go`**

**Commands:**
- `nurge account` - Show login status
- `nurge account login` - Start OAuth flow
- `nurge account logout` - Clear auth
- `nurge account login` - Interactive browser-based Google OAuth

**Auth Flow (Login):**
1. Generates state parameter for CSRF protection
2. Starts local callback server (listens on random port)
3. Opens browser to Google OAuth consent URL
4. Waits for callback with authorization code (5-minute timeout)
5. Exchanges code for Google ID token
6. Sends ID token to Convex backend (youthful-lynx-653.convex.site)
7. Convex validates and returns Nurge token
8. Saves to `~/.nurge/auth.json`:
   ```json
   { "token": "nurge_token", "email": "...", "name": "...", "google_refresh_token": "..." }
   ```

**Re-auth Flow:**
- Commands call `account.EnsureAuth()` which checks for valid token
- If token missing/invalid, triggers interactive login
- Also supports silent re-auth using stored Google refresh token

---

### **4. CONNECTOR SETUP FLOW (Gmail & LinkedIn)**

**Files: `cmd/connect.go`, `cmd/connectors.go`, `cmd/disconnect.go`**

**Gmail Connection** (`nurge connect gmail`):
1. Load user auth token
2. Call Convex endpoint: `client.ConnectGmail(token)` 
3. Convex returns connection URL or status
4. If already connected, save to config and return
5. Otherwise, open browser to connection URL
6. Poll `client.ConnectionStatus()` every 2 seconds (5-min timeout)
7. Once connected, save to config: `SetConnector("gmail", "email", email@domain)`
8. Store token at `.nurge/connectors/gmail/`

**LinkedIn Connection** (`nurge connect linkedin`):
- Same flow but for LinkedIn
- Stores as `SetConnector("linkedin", "social", profile_name)`

**Connectors Management:**
- `nurge connectors` - List connected services
- `nurge connectors available` - Show all available connectors (built-in + project-local)
- `nurge connectors info <id>` - Show connector details
- `nurge connectors add --file <yaml>` - Install custom connector definition
- `nurge disconnect <connector>` - Remove connector (deletes token + config entry)

---

### **5. EMAIL SYNC FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/sync.go`, `internal/email/sync.go`**

**Command: `nurge sync`**

1. Open database, load auth token
2. Query last sync version from `connectors` table (stores float64)
3. Call `client.SyncEmails(token, lastVersion)` to Convex
4. Convex returns batch of emails with metadata (90 days on first sync, incremental after)
5. Convert to `email.RawMessage` objects:
   ```go
   struct {
       ID, Source, Direction, ThreadID, Subject,
       FromEmail, ToEmails, CcEmails, Date, Snippet
   }
   ```
6. Call `email.StoreConvexEmails(d, raws)` Ôćĺ Stores in DB with classification
7. Update sync cursor in `connectors` table
8. Display summary: "Ôťô Synced 123 emails (45 fetched) in 2s"

**Email Storage:**
- Table: `emails` with fields: `id, source, direction, from_email, to_emails, subject, class, ...`
- Classification: `class` column (outbound, inbound, internal, transactional, newsletter, etc.)

---

### **6. EMAIL CLASSIFICATION FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/emails.go`, `internal/email/classify.go`**

**Commands:**
- `nurge emails list` - List synced emails (with optional filtering/format)
- `nurge emails stats` - Show email statistics by class
- `nurge emails classify` - Update classifications (JSON from stdin)
- `nurge emails classify-auto` - Auto-classify obvious emails (heuristics)
- `nurge emails check` - Show sending health metrics

**Classification Input** (JSON array):
```json
[
  { "id": "msg-123", "class": "outbound", "confidence": 0.95 },
  { "id": "msg-124", "class": "follow_up" }
]
```

**Auto-Classification Heuristics** (`email.ClassifyAuto`):
- Same domain as sender Ôćĺ `internal`
- noreply/no-reply addresses Ôćĺ `transactional`
- Newsletter/unsubscribe signals Ôćĺ `newsletter`
- Personal domain to personal Ôćĺ `personal`
- Infers domain from first email to detect internal

**Output:** Summary of classified count + breakdown by class

---

### **7. LEARNING & CONVERSATION CAPTURE FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/learn.go`, `internal/session/session.go`**

**Commands:**
- `nurge learn save --type <type>` - Save learning from stdin
- `nurge learn list [--type <type>]` - List all learnings
- `nurge learn distill [--dry-run]` - Distill learnings into knowledge atoms

**Learning Types:**
- `tone` - Voice/communication style
- `icp` - Ideal customer profile
- `product` - Product positioning
- `pattern` - Common sales patterns
- `competitor` - Competitive intelligence
- `preference` - Personal preferences

**Save Flow:**
1. Read content from stdin
2. Call `session.SaveLearning(d, type, content, session_id)`
3. Stores in `learnings` table with `created_at` timestamp
4. Returns learning ID

**Distill Flow** (`nurge learn distill`):
1. Query all undistilled learnings
2. For each learning, append to relevant knowledge atom
3. Update `strategic_memory` table
4. Mark learnings as distilled
5. Return summary: learnings processed, atoms created/updated

---

### **8. SKILLS GENERATION & MANAGEMENT FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/skills.go`, `cmd/skills_install.go`, `internal/session/skills.go`**

**Commands:**
- `nurge skills list` - List generated skills with versions
- `nurge skills history <name>` - Show version history  
- `nurge skills rollback <name> <version>` - Restore previous version
- `nurge skills save <name>` - Save new skill version from stdin
- `nurge skills write` - Write multiple skills from JSON array
- `nurge skills stale` - List skills needing regeneration
- `nurge skills install` - Install skill template (hidden command)

**Write Flow** (`nurge skills write`):
1. Read JSON array from stdin:
   ```json
   [
     { "name": "outbound", "content": "---\nname: nurge-outbound\n..." },
     { "name": "followup", "content": "..." }
   ]
   ```
2. Write each to disk: `.claude/skills/nurge-{name}/SKILL.md`
3. Save version to DB tracking email/learning counts at time of save
4. Return summary: "Ôťô Wrote 2 skills: nurge-outbound, nurge-followup"

**Version Tracking:**
- Each skill version stored with snapshot of email count + learning count at save time
- Used to detect when skill data has changed
- Allows rollback to previous versions

**Stale Skills Detection:**
- If new learnings added since skill saved, mark as stale
- If significant new emails classified, mark stale
- Suggest regeneration to Claude

---

### **9. CONTEXT ASSEMBLY & KNOWLEDGE FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/context.go`, `cmd/knowledge.go`, `internal/assembly/assembly.go`**

**Knowledge Atoms** - Structured learning units:
- Types: `tone`, `hooks`, `positioning`, `personas`, `objections`, `timing`, `compliance`, `structure`
- Scope: Global or scoped (persona name, tier, geography)
- Each atom has confidence score + usage count

**Knowledge Commands:**
- `nurge knowledge seed --atom <type>` - Store knowledge atom from JSON stdin
- `nurge knowledge list [--atom <type>]` - List all atoms
- `nurge knowledge query --atom <type> [--scope <scope>]` - Query atoms

**Context Assembly** (`nurge context assemble --task <task> [--prospect email]`):
Tasks:
- `outbound`, `followup`, `reply`, `research`, `qualify`, `sequence`
- `pipeline`, `analytics`, `prospects`, `product`, `icp`, `personas`
- `book`, `hygiene`, `sending_health`, `review`

Flow:
1. Parse task type
2. Load knowledge atoms for task
3. Load contact data (if prospect specified)
4. Load email history/patterns
5. Load strategic memory
6. Assemble markdown context prompt
7. Output to stdout (or JSON)

**Session Resume** (`nurge context resume`):
- Returns compact working-state summary (~200 tokens)
- Active sequences, pending replies, due follow-ups, recent learnings
- Used for session hook injection

---

### **10. SEQUENCE & FOLLOW-UP FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/sequence.go`, `cmd/send.go`, `cmd/dispatcher.go`, `cmd/tasks.go`, `internal/sequence/`**

**Data Structures:**
- **Enrollment**: A prospect enrolled in a sequence template
- **Task**: A follow-up step scheduled to send
- **Template**: Multi-step sequence with delays and send types

**Create Enrollment:**
- User sends email with `--sequence <template-name>`
- `nurge send --to email --subject ... --body ... --sequence "3-touch" --campaign "Q1 2024"`
- Creates enrollment record in DB
- Enrolls in template steps (step 2+ are future tasks)

**Task Management:**
- `nurge tasks list` - Show due follow-up tasks (from sequences or inferred)
- `nurge tasks snooze <task-id> --days 3` - Postpone task
- `nurge tasks cancel <task-id> [--reason "..."]` - Cancel task

**Sequences:**
- `nurge sequence list [--status active|paused|completed]` - List enrollments
- `nurge sequence pause <enrollment-id>` - Pause enrollment
- `nurge sequence resume <enrollment-id>` - Resume paused enrollment
- `nurge sequence templates` - List available templates
- `nurge sequence templates add --file template.json` - Add custom template
- `nurge sequence templates delete <name>` - Delete custom template

**Template Format:**
```json
{
  "name": "3-touch",
  "steps": [
    { "step_number": 1, "name": "Cold Outreach", "send_type": "first_touch", "delay": "0d" },
    { "step_number": 2, "name": "Follow-up 1", "send_type": "follow_up", "delay": "3d" },
    { "step_number": 3, "name": "Follow-up 2", "send_type": "follow_up", "delay": "7d" }
  ]
}
```

---

### **11. SEND FLOW (Email Sending)**

**Files: `cmd/send.go`, `internal/email/send.go`**

**Command: `nurge send --to email --subject ... --body ... [options]`**

**Options:**
- `--to` - Recipient email (required)
- `--cc` - CC address
- `--subject` - Email subject (required)
- `--body` - Email body plain text (required)
- `--sequence <template>` - Enroll in sequence
- `--campaign <name>` - Campaign name for tracking
- `--task-id <id>` - Mark existing task as sent
- `--send-type <type>` - Override classification: first_touch, follow_up, reply, manual

**Send Flow:**
1. Load auth token
2. If `--task-id`, resolve from DB and fill missing fields
3. Validate required fields
4. If `--sequence`, build sequence steps from template
5. Call `client.SendEmail(token, request)` to Convex
6. Convex queues/sends via Unipile (email provider)
7. Response includes `MessageID`, `TaskID`, `EnrollmentID`
8. Display: "Ôťô Sent to email@domain (message ID: ...)" or "ÔĆ│ Queued..."

**Sequence Enrollment Flow:**
- If `--sequence`, first step is the current send
- Steps 2+ become scheduled tasks with delays
- Tasks stored in `sequence_tasks` table

---

### **12. DISPATCHER & AUTOMATION FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/dispatcher.go`, `internal/sequence/dispatch.go`**

**Command: `nurge dispatcher run [--sync-replies] [--execute] [--transport-email email]`**

**Dispatcher Run Flow:**
1. If `--sync-replies`, call `email.SyncReplies()` to check for responses
2. Query due tasks from `sequence_tasks` where due_at <= now
3. Evaluate against mailbox policy (daily caps, send windows, warmup stage)
4. If `--execute`, render and send tasks via Gmail API
5. For each sent task:
   - Update `sequence_tasks` status to `sent`
   - Record `MessageID`, `ThreadID`
   - Advance enrollment to next step
   - Log send attempt

**Output:**
- `ready` - Task can be sent
- `blocked` - Task blocked by policy (show reason)
- Display each task: recipient, due time, subject preview
- If executed: show results (sent/failed) with errors

**Install Unattended Dispatcher** (`nurge dispatcher install`):
- Generates wrapper script that runs `nurge dispatcher run --execute`
- Creates launchd plist for macOS
- Generates cron line for Linux
- Installs in `.nurge/automation/`

---

### **13. ENRICHMENT FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/enrich.go`, `internal/enrich/`**

**Commands:**
- `nurge enrich --stats` - Show enrichment coverage
- `nurge enrich --prospect <email>` - Enrich single contact
- `nurge enrich --batch [--limit 50]` - Enrich unenriched contacts
- `nurge enrich company <linkedin-slug>` - Get company profile + employees
- `nurge enrich person <linkedin-identifier>` - Get full LinkedIn profile
- `nurge enrich search --keywords "VP Sales" --company <id>` - Search LinkedIn
- `nurge enrich hn <company>` - Search Hacker News mentions
- `nurge enrich github <org>` - Fetch GitHub org data
- `nurge enrich save --stdin` - Save enrichment results from JSON

**Enrichment Waterfall** (for prospects):
1. Check DB cache for existing enrichment
2. Call external providers in order (configurable YAML)
3. Merge results into contact record
4. Cache results
5. Return: filled fields + missing fields

**Enrichable Fields:**
- `name`, `title`, `company`, `phone`, `email`, `linkedin_url`, etc.

**Company Enrichment Flow:**
1. Extract LinkedIn slug from URL or use directly
2. Call Convex to fetch LinkedIn data via Unipile
3. Call `enrich.SearchHackerNews(slug, limit)`
4. Call `enrich.FetchGitHubOrg(slug)`
5. Call `enrich.FetchWikipedia(slug)`
6. Combine results into single JSON
7. Print human-readable summary with employees, repos, mentions

---

### **14. RESOLVE FLOW (Contact Resolution)**

**Files: `cmd/resolve.go`, `internal/resolve/`**

**Command: `nurge resolve <input> [--company name] [--file contacts.csv]`**

**Input Types:**
- Email: `john@acme.com`
- LinkedIn URL: `linkedin.com/in/john-doe` or `linkedin.com/company/acme`
- Website: `acme.com`
- Name + Company: `"John Doe" --company "Acme"`
- CSV File: `--file contacts.csv`

**Resolve Flow:**
1. Detect input type
2. If email, look up in DB or enrich
3. If LinkedIn, call Convex to fetch
4. If company domain, extract company info
5. If name + company, resolve to contact
6. Return: matched company + contacts list with status (found/enriched/pending)

**Output:**
- Summary line (type detected, next steps)
- Company info (if found)
- Contacts list with enrichment status + missing fields

---

### **15. METRICS & ANALYTICS FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/metrics.go`, `internal/metrics/`**

All commands log to metrics table: `command_name`, `output_size_bytes`, `timestamp`

**Command: `nurge metrics`** - Show usage statistics

---

### **16. CLAUDE CODE HOOKS INTEGRATION**

**Files: `cmd/hook.go`, `internal/project/step_hooks.go`, `internal/sync/`**

**Hooks Installed:**
- `session-start` - Print banner and context
- `user-prompt` - Capture user input for learning
- `stop` - Checkpoint current work
- `session-end` - Mark session ended
- `post-tool-use` - Reserved for future

**Session Start Hook Output:**
- Project status (initialized, setup completed)
- Connector statuses
- Email summary (total, outbound count)
- Weekly sending stats
- Skills list + stale skills warning
- Contact count, learning count
- Recent strategic memory
- Sync attempt report

---

### **KEY INTERNAL PACKAGES**

**`internal/account/`** - Auth, Convex API client, Google OAuth
**`internal/db/`** - SQLite database schema + migrations
**`internal/email/`** - Email storage, sync, classification, sending
**`internal/project/`** - Project init, config management
**`internal/sequence/`** - Enrollments, tasks, templates, dispatch logic
**`internal/session/`** - Learnings, skills versioning, strategic memory
**`internal/assembly/`** - Knowledge atoms, context assembly
**`internal/enrich/`** - Contact enrichment via multiple sources
**`internal/resolve/`** - Contact resolution from various inputs
**`internal/connector/`** - Connector registry + YAML parsing
**`internal/automation/`** - Dispatcher setup, cron/launchd generation

---

### **COMMAND HIERARCHY**

```
nurge (init)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ account
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ login
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ logout

---

### **DATA PERSISTENCE LAYERS**

1. **Auth**: `~/.nurge/auth.json` (user token, Google refresh token)
2. **Config**: `.nurge/config.json` (connectors, settings)
3. **Database**: `.nurge/nurge.db` (SQLite) - all project state
4. **Connectors**: `.nurge/connectors/<connector-id>/` (token files)
5. **Skills**: `.claude/skills/nurge-<name>/SKILL.md`
6. **Hooks**: `.claude/hooks/` (installed hook scripts)

---

### **COMPLETE USER JOURNEY EXAMPLE**

```bash
# 1. Initialize project
nurge my-outbound

# 2. Login (one-time, browser OAuth)
nurge account login

# 3. Connect Gmail
nurge connect gmail

# 4. Sync past emails (90 days initially)
nurge sync

# 5. View and classify emails
nurge emails list --limit 10
# Ôćĺ Claude helps classify as outbound/reply/internal/etc

# 6. Start learning
nurge learn save --type icp < icp.txt
nurge learn save --type tone < tone.txt
nurge learn distill

# 7. Generate skills
# Claude in Claude Code uses context to generate nurge-outbound, nurge-follow-up skills
nurge skills write < skills.json

# 8. Create sequence template
nurge sequence templates add --file template.json

# 9. Send first email + enroll in sequence
nurge send --to prospect@company.com --subject "..." --body "..." --sequence "3-touch" --campaign "Q1 2024"

# 10. Dispatcher checks due tasks periodically
nurge dispatcher run --sync-replies --execute

# 11. View tasks and enrichment
nurge tasks list
nurge enrich --prospect prospect@company.com
```

This is the complete architecture and flow of Nurge from the actual codebase.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information to write a comprehensive summary. Let me create the complete user flow document:

## NURGE COMPLETE USER FLOW - CODE ANALYSIS

Based on thorough exploration of the codebase, here is the complete user flow from actual implementation:

---

### **1. ENTRY POINT & ROOT COMMAND FLOW**

**File: `/cmd/root.go` and `main.go`**

**Entry:** `main()` Ôćĺ Sets build info Ôćĺ Calls `cmd.Execute()`

**Root Command Behavior:**
- `nurge [directory]` - shorthand for `nurge init [directory]`
- If first arg is not a known command/flag, rewrites `os.Args` to inject `init` 
- Pre-run hooks check for auth (skipped for `init`, `account login/logout`, `help`, `version`)
- Metrics logged for every command (except `init`, `metrics`, `version`)
- Update check runs async (2s timeout) and shows notification on stderr

---

### **2. INITIALIZATION FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/init.go` and `internal/project/init.go`, `internal/project/step_*.go`**

When user runs `nurge <directory>` or `nurge init [directory]`:

1. **Directory Setup** (runInit in root.go)
   - Creates directory if provided, changes to it
   - Validates directory is empty (unless `--force`)
   
2. **Project Initialization Steps** (Run() in init.go) - sequential execution:
   - **projectStep**: Creates `.nurge/` directory, initializes `config.json`
   - **databaseStep**: Creates SQLite database at `.nurge/nurge.db` with schema
   - **gitignoreStep**: Writes `.gitignore` to exclude `.nurge/`
   - **hooksStep** (optional, `--skip-hooks`): Installs Claude Code hooks
   - **skillStep**: Generates initial global setup skill in `.claude/skills/`
   - **gitStep**: Initializes git repository
   - **nextStepStep**: Prints next steps to user

**Config Structure** (`config.json`):
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "created_at": "RFC3339_timestamp",
  "connectors": { "id": { "connected": bool, "account": "email@domain" } },
  "setup_completed": false,
  "settings": {}
}
```

**Database**: `.nurge/nurge.db` (SQLite) - stores all project state

---

### **3. AUTH/ACCOUNT FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/account.go`, `internal/account/auth.go`, `internal/account/convex.go`**

**Commands:**
- `nurge account` - Show login status
- `nurge account login` - Start OAuth flow
- `nurge account logout` - Clear auth
- `nurge account login` - Interactive browser-based Google OAuth

**Auth Flow (Login):**
1. Generates state parameter for CSRF protection
2. Starts local callback server (listens on random port)
3. Opens browser to Google OAuth consent URL
4. Waits for callback with authorization code (5-minute timeout)
5. Exchanges code for Google ID token
6. Sends ID token to Convex backend (youthful-lynx-653.convex.site)
7. Convex validates and returns Nurge token
8. Saves to `~/.nurge/auth.json`:
   ```json
   { "token": "nurge_token", "email": "...", "name": "...", "google_refresh_token": "..." }
   ```

**Re-auth Flow:**
- Commands call `account.EnsureAuth()` which checks for valid token
- If token missing/invalid, triggers interactive login
- Also supports silent re-auth using stored Google refresh token

---

### **4. CONNECTOR SETUP FLOW (Gmail & LinkedIn)**

**Files: `cmd/connect.go`, `cmd/connectors.go`, `cmd/disconnect.go`**

**Gmail Connection** (`nurge connect gmail`):
1. Load user auth token
2. Call Convex endpoint: `client.ConnectGmail(token)` 
3. Convex returns connection URL or status
4. If already connected, save to config and return
5. Otherwise, open browser to connection URL
6. Poll `client.ConnectionStatus()` every 2 seconds (5-min timeout)
7. Once connected, save to config: `SetConnector("gmail", "email", email@domain)`
8. Store token at `.nurge/connectors/gmail/`

**LinkedIn Connection** (`nurge connect linkedin`):
- Same flow but for LinkedIn
- Stores as `SetConnector("linkedin", "social", profile_name)`

**Connectors Management:**
- `nurge connectors` - List connected services
- `nurge connectors available` - Show all available connectors (built-in + project-local)
- `nurge connectors info <id>` - Show connector details
- `nurge connectors add --file <yaml>` - Install custom connector definition
- `nurge disconnect <connector>` - Remove connector (deletes token + config entry)

---

### **5. EMAIL SYNC FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/sync.go`, `internal/email/sync.go`**

**Command: `nurge sync`**

1. Open database, load auth token
2. Query last sync version from `connectors` table (stores float64)
3. Call `client.SyncEmails(token, lastVersion)` to Convex
4. Convex returns batch of emails with metadata (90 days on first sync, incremental after)
5. Convert to `email.RawMessage` objects:
   ```go
   struct {
       ID, Source, Direction, ThreadID, Subject,
       FromEmail, ToEmails, CcEmails, Date, Snippet
   }
   ```
6. Call `email.StoreConvexEmails(d, raws)` Ôćĺ Stores in DB with classification
7. Update sync cursor in `connectors` table
8. Display summary: "Ôťô Synced 123 emails (45 fetched) in 2s"

**Email Storage:**
- Table: `emails` with fields: `id, source, direction, from_email, to_emails, subject, class, ...`
- Classification: `class` column (outbound, inbound, internal, transactional, newsletter, etc.)

---

### **6. EMAIL CLASSIFICATION FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/emails.go`, `internal/email/classify.go`**

**Commands:**
- `nurge emails list` - List synced emails (with optional filtering/format)
- `nurge emails stats` - Show email statistics by class
- `nurge emails classify` - Update classifications (JSON from stdin)
- `nurge emails classify-auto` - Auto-classify obvious emails (heuristics)
- `nurge emails check` - Show sending health metrics

**Classification Input** (JSON array):
```json
[
  { "id": "msg-123", "class": "outbound", "confidence": 0.95 },
  { "id": "msg-124", "class": "follow_up" }
]
```

**Auto-Classification Heuristics** (`email.ClassifyAuto`):
- Same domain as sender Ôćĺ `internal`
- noreply/no-reply addresses Ôćĺ `transactional`
- Newsletter/unsubscribe signals Ôćĺ `newsletter`
- Personal domain to personal Ôćĺ `personal`
- Infers domain from first email to detect internal

**Output:** Summary of classified count + breakdown by class

---

### **7. LEARNING & CONVERSATION CAPTURE FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/learn.go`, `internal/session/session.go`**

**Commands:**
- `nurge learn save --type <type>` - Save learning from stdin
- `nurge learn list [--type <type>]` - List all learnings
- `nurge learn distill [--dry-run]` - Distill learnings into knowledge atoms

**Learning Types:**
- `tone` - Voice/communication style
- `icp` - Ideal customer profile
- `product` - Product positioning
- `pattern` - Common sales patterns
- `competitor` - Competitive intelligence
- `preference` - Personal preferences

**Save Flow:**
1. Read content from stdin
2. Call `session.SaveLearning(d, type, content, session_id)`
3. Stores in `learnings` table with `created_at` timestamp
4. Returns learning ID

**Distill Flow** (`nurge learn distill`):
1. Query all undistilled learnings
2. For each learning, append to relevant knowledge atom
3. Update `strategic_memory` table
4. Mark learnings as distilled
5. Return summary: learnings processed, atoms created/updated

---

### **8. SKILLS GENERATION & MANAGEMENT FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/skills.go`, `cmd/skills_install.go`, `internal/session/skills.go`**

**Commands:**
- `nurge skills list` - List generated skills with versions
- `nurge skills history <name>` - Show version history  
- `nurge skills rollback <name> <version>` - Restore previous version
- `nurge skills save <name>` - Save new skill version from stdin
- `nurge skills write` - Write multiple skills from JSON array
- `nurge skills stale` - List skills needing regeneration
- `nurge skills install` - Install skill template (hidden command)

**Write Flow** (`nurge skills write`):
1. Read JSON array from stdin:
   ```json
   [
     { "name": "outbound", "content": "---\nname: nurge-outbound\n..." },
     { "name": "followup", "content": "..." }
   ]
   ```
2. Write each to disk: `.claude/skills/nurge-{name}/SKILL.md`
3. Save version to DB tracking email/learning counts at time of save
4. Return summary: "Ôťô Wrote 2 skills: nurge-outbound, nurge-followup"

**Version Tracking:**
- Each skill version stored with snapshot of email count + learning count at save time
- Used to detect when skill data has changed
- Allows rollback to previous versions

**Stale Skills Detection:**
- If new learnings added since skill saved, mark as stale
- If significant new emails classified, mark stale
- Suggest regeneration to Claude

---

### **9. CONTEXT ASSEMBLY & KNOWLEDGE FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/context.go`, `cmd/knowledge.go`, `internal/assembly/assembly.go`**

**Knowledge Atoms** - Structured learning units:
- Types: `tone`, `hooks`, `positioning`, `personas`, `objections`, `timing`, `compliance`, `structure`
- Scope: Global or scoped (persona name, tier, geography)
- Each atom has confidence score + usage count

**Knowledge Commands:**
- `nurge knowledge seed --atom <type>` - Store knowledge atom from JSON stdin
- `nurge knowledge list [--atom <type>]` - List all atoms
- `nurge knowledge query --atom <type> [--scope <scope>]` - Query atoms

**Context Assembly** (`nurge context assemble --task <task> [--prospect email]`):
Tasks:
- `outbound`, `followup`, `reply`, `research`, `qualify`, `sequence`
- `pipeline`, `analytics`, `prospects`, `product`, `icp`, `personas`
- `book`, `hygiene`, `sending_health`, `review`

Flow:
1. Parse task type
2. Load knowledge atoms for task
3. Load contact data (if prospect specified)
4. Load email history/patterns
5. Load strategic memory
6. Assemble markdown context prompt
7. Output to stdout (or JSON)

**Session Resume** (`nurge context resume`):
- Returns compact working-state summary (~200 tokens)
- Active sequences, pending replies, due follow-ups, recent learnings
- Used for session hook injection

---

### **10. SEQUENCE & FOLLOW-UP FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/sequence.go`, `cmd/send.go`, `cmd/dispatcher.go`, `cmd/tasks.go`, `internal/sequence/`**

**Data Structures:**
- **Enrollment**: A prospect enrolled in a sequence template
- **Task**: A follow-up step scheduled to send
- **Template**: Multi-step sequence with delays and send types

**Create Enrollment:**
- User sends email with `--sequence <template-name>`
- `nurge send --to email --subject ... --body ... --sequence "3-touch" --campaign "Q1 2024"`
- Creates enrollment record in DB
- Enrolls in template steps (step 2+ are future tasks)

**Task Management:**
- `nurge tasks list` - Show due follow-up tasks (from sequences or inferred)
- `nurge tasks snooze <task-id> --days 3` - Postpone task
- `nurge tasks cancel <task-id> [--reason "..."]` - Cancel task

**Sequences:**
- `nurge sequence list [--status active|paused|completed]` - List enrollments
- `nurge sequence pause <enrollment-id>` - Pause enrollment
- `nurge sequence resume <enrollment-id>` - Resume paused enrollment
- `nurge sequence templates` - List available templates
- `nurge sequence templates add --file template.json` - Add custom template
- `nurge sequence templates delete <name>` - Delete custom template

**Template Format:**
```json
{
  "name": "3-touch",
  "steps": [
    { "step_number": 1, "name": "Cold Outreach", "send_type": "first_touch", "delay": "0d" },
    { "step_number": 2, "name": "Follow-up 1", "send_type": "follow_up", "delay": "3d" },
    { "step_number": 3, "name": "Follow-up 2", "send_type": "follow_up", "delay": "7d" }
  ]
}
```

---

### **11. SEND FLOW (Email Sending)**

**Files: `cmd/send.go`, `internal/email/send.go`**

**Command: `nurge send --to email --subject ... --body ... [options]`**

**Options:**
- `--to` - Recipient email (required)
- `--cc` - CC address
- `--subject` - Email subject (required)
- `--body` - Email body plain text (required)
- `--sequence <template>` - Enroll in sequence
- `--campaign <name>` - Campaign name for tracking
- `--task-id <id>` - Mark existing task as sent
- `--send-type <type>` - Override classification: first_touch, follow_up, reply, manual

**Send Flow:**
1. Load auth token
2. If `--task-id`, resolve from DB and fill missing fields
3. Validate required fields
4. If `--sequence`, build sequence steps from template
5. Call `client.SendEmail(token, request)` to Convex
6. Convex queues/sends via Unipile (email provider)
7. Response includes `MessageID`, `TaskID`, `EnrollmentID`
8. Display: "Ôťô Sent to email@domain (message ID: ...)" or "ÔĆ│ Queued..."

**Sequence Enrollment Flow:**
- If `--sequence`, first step is the current send
- Steps 2+ become scheduled tasks with delays
- Tasks stored in `sequence_tasks` table

---

### **12. DISPATCHER & AUTOMATION FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/dispatcher.go`, `internal/sequence/dispatch.go`**

**Command: `nurge dispatcher run [--sync-replies] [--execute] [--transport-email email]`**

**Dispatcher Run Flow:**
1. If `--sync-replies`, call `email.SyncReplies()` to check for responses
2. Query due tasks from `sequence_tasks` where due_at <= now
3. Evaluate against mailbox policy (daily caps, send windows, warmup stage)
4. If `--execute`, render and send tasks via Gmail API
5. For each sent task:
   - Update `sequence_tasks` status to `sent`
   - Record `MessageID`, `ThreadID`
   - Advance enrollment to next step
   - Log send attempt

**Output:**
- `ready` - Task can be sent
- `blocked` - Task blocked by policy (show reason)
- Display each task: recipient, due time, subject preview
- If executed: show results (sent/failed) with errors

**Install Unattended Dispatcher** (`nurge dispatcher install`):
- Generates wrapper script that runs `nurge dispatcher run --execute`
- Creates launchd plist for macOS
- Generates cron line for Linux
- Installs in `.nurge/automation/`

---

### **13. ENRICHMENT FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/enrich.go`, `internal/enrich/`**

**Commands:**
- `nurge enrich --stats` - Show enrichment coverage
- `nurge enrich --prospect <email>` - Enrich single contact
- `nurge enrich --batch [--limit 50]` - Enrich unenriched contacts
- `nurge enrich company <linkedin-slug>` - Get company profile + employees
- `nurge enrich person <linkedin-identifier>` - Get full LinkedIn profile
- `nurge enrich search --keywords "VP Sales" --company <id>` - Search LinkedIn
- `nurge enrich hn <company>` - Search Hacker News mentions
- `nurge enrich github <org>` - Fetch GitHub org data
- `nurge enrich save --stdin` - Save enrichment results from JSON

**Enrichment Waterfall** (for prospects):
1. Check DB cache for existing enrichment
2. Call external providers in order (configurable YAML)
3. Merge results into contact record
4. Cache results
5. Return: filled fields + missing fields

**Enrichable Fields:**
- `name`, `title`, `company`, `phone`, `email`, `linkedin_url`, etc.

**Company Enrichment Flow:**
1. Extract LinkedIn slug from URL or use directly
2. Call Convex to fetch LinkedIn data via Unipile
3. Call `enrich.SearchHackerNews(slug, limit)`
4. Call `enrich.FetchGitHubOrg(slug)`
5. Call `enrich.FetchWikipedia(slug)`
6. Combine results into single JSON
7. Print human-readable summary with employees, repos, mentions

---

### **14. RESOLVE FLOW (Contact Resolution)**

**Files: `cmd/resolve.go`, `internal/resolve/`**

**Command: `nurge resolve <input> [--company name] [--file contacts.csv]`**

**Input Types:**
- Email: `john@acme.com`
- LinkedIn URL: `linkedin.com/in/john-doe` or `linkedin.com/company/acme`
- Website: `acme.com`
- Name + Company: `"John Doe" --company "Acme"`
- CSV File: `--file contacts.csv`

**Resolve Flow:**
1. Detect input type
2. If email, look up in DB or enrich
3. If LinkedIn, call Convex to fetch
4. If company domain, extract company info
5. If name + company, resolve to contact
6. Return: matched company + contacts list with status (found/enriched/pending)

**Output:**
- Summary line (type detected, next steps)
- Company info (if found)
- Contacts list with enrichment status + missing fields

---

### **15. METRICS & ANALYTICS FLOW**

**Files: `cmd/metrics.go`, `internal/metrics/`**

All commands log to metrics table: `command_name`, `output_size_bytes`, `timestamp`

**Command: `nurge metrics`** - Show usage statistics

---

### **16. CLAUDE CODE HOOKS INTEGRATION**

**Files: `cmd/hook.go`, `internal/project/step_hooks.go`, `internal/sync/`**

**Hooks Installed:**
- `session-start` - Print banner and context
- `user-prompt` - Capture user input for learning
- `stop` - Checkpoint current work
- `session-end` - Mark session ended
- `post-tool-use` - Reserved for future

**Session Start Hook Output:**
- Project status (initialized, setup completed)
- Connector statuses
- Email summary (total, outbound count)
- Weekly sending stats
- Skills list + stale skills warning
- Contact count, learning count
- Recent strategic memory
- Sync attempt report

---

### **KEY INTERNAL PACKAGES**

**`internal/account/`** - Auth, Convex API client, Google OAuth
**`internal/db/`** - SQLite database schema + migrations
**`internal/email/`** - Email storage, sync, classification, sending
**`internal/project/`** - Project init, config management
**`internal/sequence/`** - Enrollments, tasks, templates, dispatch logic
**`internal/session/`** - Learnings, skills versioning, strategic memory
**`internal/assembly/`** - Knowledge atoms, context assembly
**`internal/enrich/`** - Contact enrichment via multiple sources
**`internal/resolve/`** - Contact resolution from various inputs
**`internal/connector/`** - Connector registry + YAML parsing
**`internal/automation/`** - Dispatcher setup, cron/launchd generation

---

### **COMMAND HIERARCHY**

```
nurge (init)
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ account
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ login
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ logout

ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ disconnect <connector>

ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ mailboxes

Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ post-tool-use
```

---

### **DATA PERSISTENCE LAYERS**

1. **Auth**: `~/.nurge/auth.json` (user token, Google refresh token)
2. **Config**: `.nurge/config.json` (connectors, settings)
3. **Database**: `.nurge/nurge.db` (SQLite) - all project state
4. **Connectors**: `.nurge/connectors/<connector-id>/` (token files)
5. **Skills**: `.claude/skills/nurge-<name>/SKILL.md`
6. **Hooks**: `.claude/hooks/` (installed hook scripts)

---

### **COMPLETE USER JOURNEY EXAMPLE**

```bash
# 1. Initialize project
nurge my-outbound

# 2. Login (one-time, browser OAuth)
nurge account login

# 3. Connect Gmail
nurge connect gmail

# 4. Sync past emails (90 days initially)
nurge sync

# 5. View and classify emails
nurge emails list --limit 10
# Ôćĺ Claude helps classify as outbound/reply/internal/etc

# 6. Start learning
nurge learn save --type icp < icp.txt
nurge learn save --type tone < tone.txt
nurge learn distill

# 7. Generate skills
# Claude in Claude Code uses context to generate nurge-outbound, nurge-follow-up skills
nurge skills write < skills.json

# 8. Create sequence template
nurge sequence templates add --file template.json

# 9. Send first email + enroll in sequence
nurge send --to prospect@company.com --subject "..." --body "..." --sequence "3-touch" --campaign "Q1 2024"

# 10. Dispatcher checks due tasks periodically
nurge dispatcher run --sync-replies --execute

# 11. View tasks and enrichment
nurge tasks list
nurge enrich --prospect prospect@company.com
```

This is the complete architecture and flow of Nurge from the actual codebase.

Here's the complete Nurge user flow, traced from code:

---

## Complete User Flow

### Phase 1: Install & Init

```
curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash    # installs Go binary
nurge my-outbound                               # creates project directory
```

**`nurge <directory>`** (cmd/root.go) rewrites args to `nurge init <directory>` if first arg isn't a known command. Init runs these steps sequentially (internal/project/step_*.go):

1. **projectStep** - creates `.nurge/` dir + `config.json`
2. **databaseStep** - creates SQLite DB at `.nurge/nurge.db`
3. **gitignoreStep** - writes `.gitignore` excluding `.nurge/`
4. **hooksStep** - installs Claude Code hooks (skippable with `--skip-hooks`)
5. **skillStep** - installs setup skill to `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/`
6. **gitStep** - `git init` + initial commit
7. **nextStepStep** - detects environment, prints "run /nurge:setup"

### Phase 2: Account Auth

```
nurge account login
```

1. Generates CSRF state param
2. Starts local callback server on random port
3. Opens browser to Google OAuth consent
4. Waits for callback (5-min timeout)
5. Exchanges code for Google ID token
6. Sends to Convex backend (`youthful-lynx-653.convex.site`)
7. Saves token to `~/.nurge/auth.json`

All subsequent commands call `account.EnsureAuth()` in pre-run hook (skipped for init/version/help).

### Phase 3: Connect Services

```
nurge connect gmail
nurge connect linkedin    # optional
```

1. Calls Convex with auth token
2. If not already connected, opens browser to OAuth
3. Polls `client.ConnectionStatus()` every 2s (5-min timeout)
4. Saves connector state to `config.json` + token to `.nurge/connectors/<id>/`

### Phase 4: Sync Emails

```
nurge sync
```

1. Reads last sync cursor from `connectors` table
2. Calls `client.SyncEmails(token, lastVersion)` to Convex
3. First sync: 90 days of sent emails. After: incremental
4. Stores as `email.RawMessage` in DB (id, subject, from, to, cc, date, snippet, class)
5. Prints summary: "Synced 123 emails (45 fetched) in 2s"

### Phase 5: Classify Emails

```
nurge emails classify-auto           # heuristic auto-classify
nurge emails classify --stdin        # Claude-driven classification
```

**Auto-classify heuristics:**
- Same domain as sender -> `internal`
- noreply/no-reply -> `transactional`
- Unsubscribe signals -> `newsletter`
- Personal domain -> `personal`

**Claude classification** receives JSON from stdin:
```json
[{"id": "msg-123", "class": "outbound", "confidence": 0.95}]
```

Other email commands:
- `nurge emails list [--class outbound] [--limit 50] [--compact]`
- `nurge emails stats` - breakdown by class
- `nurge emails check` - sending health metrics

### Phase 6: Learning & Knowledge

```
nurge learn save --type tone         # stdin: tone observations
nurge learn save --type icp          # stdin: ICP refinements
nurge learn save --type product      # stdin: product positioning
nurge learn list --format json
nurge learn distill                  # distill into knowledge atoms
```

**Learning types:** `tone`, `icp`, `product`, `pattern`, `competitor`, `preference`

**Distill flow:** processes undistilled learnings -> creates/updates knowledge atoms -> marks as distilled.

**Knowledge atoms** (cmd/knowledge.go):
```
nurge knowledge seed --atom positioning --scope global
nurge knowledge list --atom tone
nurge knowledge query --atom personas --scope "VP Engineering"
```

Atom types: `tone`, `hooks`, `positioning`, `personas`, `objections`, `timing`, `compliance`, `structure`

### Phase 7: Context Assembly

```
nurge context assemble --task outbound --prospect john@acme.com
nurge context resume     # compact working-state (~200 tokens)
```

Assembles knowledge atoms + contact data + email history + strategic memory into a markdown prompt. Tasks: `outbound`, `followup`, `reply`, `research`, `qualify`, `sequence`, `pipeline`, `analytics`, `prospects`, `product`, `icp`, `personas`, `book`, `hygiene`, `sending_health`, `review`.

### Phase 8: Skills Generation

```
nurge skills write       # JSON array from stdin -> writes SKILL.md files
nurge skills list
nurge skills stale       # detect skills needing regen
nurge skills history <name>
nurge skills rollback <name> <version>
```

Skills written to `.claude/skills/nurge-<name>/SKILL.md`. Each version tracked in DB with email count + learning count snapshot at save time. Stale detection: new learnings or emails since last save.

### Phase 9: Prospect Research & Enrichment

```
nurge resolve john@acme.com                    # detect type, lookup
nurge resolve linkedin.com/in/john-doe         # LinkedIn profile
nurge resolve acme.com                         # company domain
nurge resolve "John Doe" --company "Acme"      # name+company

nurge enrich --prospect john@acme.com          # single contact
nurge enrich --batch --limit 50                # batch enrich
nurge enrich company acme-inc                  # LinkedIn company + employees
nurge enrich person john-doe                   # full LinkedIn profile
nurge enrich search --keywords "VP Sales" --company <id>
nurge enrich hn acme                           # Hacker News mentions
nurge enrich github acme                       # GitHub org data
nurge enrich save --stdin                      # save results
```

### Phase 10: Send & Sequences

```
nurge send --to john@acme.com --subject "..." --body "..." \
  --sequence "3-touch" --campaign "Q1 2024"
```

1. Validates fields, loads auth
2. If `--sequence`, builds steps from template (step 1 = current send, step 2+ = future tasks)
3. Calls `client.SendEmail()` to Convex -> Unipile
4. Returns MessageID + EnrollmentID

**Sequence management:**
```
nurge sequence list [--status active|paused|completed]
nurge sequence pause <enrollment-id>
nurge sequence resume <enrollment-id>
nurge sequence templates                        # list templates
nurge sequence templates add --file template.json
```

**Task management** (follow-ups from sequences):
```
nurge tasks list [--after-days 7]
nurge tasks snooze <task-id> --days 3
nurge tasks cancel <task-id> --reason "replied"
```

### Phase 11: Dispatcher (Automation)

```
nurge dispatcher run --sync-replies --execute
nurge dispatcher install --interval-minutes 30
```

**Run flow:**
1. Optionally sync replies first
2. Query due tasks where `due_at <= now`
3. Evaluate against mailbox policy (daily caps, send windows, warmup stage)
4. If `--execute`, render + send each task
5. Update task status, advance enrollment

**Install:** generates launchd plist (macOS) or cron line (Linux) for unattended runs.

### Phase 12: Mailbox Health

```
nurge mailboxes status
nurge mailboxes set <id> --daily-cap 50 --send-window "9-17"
nurge mailboxes warmup --stage 2
nurge mailboxes health --dns
```

### Hooks (Background, Automatic)

Installed in `.claude/settings.json`:

| Hook | Trigger | Action |
|------|---------|--------|
| `session-start` | Claude Code opens | Prints project status, connector states, email summary, stale skills |
| `user-prompt` | Each user message | Captures input for learning |
| `stop` | Claude stops | Checkpoints current work |
| `session-end` | Session closes | Marks session ended |

---

## Command Tree

```
nurge [directory]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ account [login|logout]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ connect <gmail|linkedin>
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ disconnect <id>
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ connectors [available|info|validate|add]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ sync
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ emails [list|stats|classify|classify-auto|check]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ send
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ tasks [list|snooze|cancel]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ sequence [list|pause|resume|templates]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ dispatcher [run|install]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ learn [save|list|distill]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ skills [list|history|rollback|save|write|stale]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ context [generate|assemble|resume]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ knowledge [seed|list|query]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ enrich [company|person|search|hn|github|save]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ resolve
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ mailboxes [status|set|warmup|health]
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ metrics
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ update
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ version
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ hook [session-start|user-prompt|stop|session-end] (hidden)
```

## Data Layers

| Layer | Location | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|
| Auth | `~/.nurge/auth.json` | User token + Google refresh |
| Config | `.nurge/config.json` | Connectors, settings |
| Database | `.nurge/nurge.db` | All project state (SQLite) |
| Connectors | `.nurge/connectors/<id>/` | Per-service tokens |
| Skills | `.claude/skills/nurge-*/SKILL.md` | Claude-readable skills |
| Hooks | `.claude/settings.json` | Hook definitions |
