1Ôćĺ# Phase 2: Zero ÔÇö Build the Prospect Intelligence Framework
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ**Who:** <200 outbound, no CRM. Starting from scratch.
     4Ôćĺ**Data source:** All intelligence comes from external research. No email history to learn from.
     5Ôćĺ**Philosophy:** Nurge does the homework. User validates.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ## Resume Keys
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ| Config key exists | Skip step |
    10Ôćĺ|-------------------|-----------|
    11Ôćĺ| `sending.domain_type` | Z1 |
    12Ôćĺ| `sending.deliverability_checked` | Z2 |
    13Ôćĺ| `prospects.geography` | Z3 |
    14Ôćĺ| `qualification.disqualifiers` | S1 |
    15Ôćĺ| `icp.company_size` | S2 |
    16Ôćĺ| `personas` | S3 |
    17Ôćĺ| `framework.transformations_seeded` | S4 |
    18Ôćĺ| `framework.signals_seeded` | S5 |
    19Ôćĺ| `framework.insights_seeded` | S6 |
    20Ôćĺ| `framework.voice_seeded` | S7 |
    21Ôćĺ| Skills exist in `.claude/skills/nurge-*/` | S9 |
    22Ôćĺ| `setup_completed` | S10 |
    23Ôćĺ
    24Ôćĺ---
    25Ôćĺ
    26Ôćĺ## Z1: Sending Infrastructure
    27Ôćĺ
    28ÔćĺAsk: "Are you sending from a dedicated outbound domain, or your main business domain?"
    29ÔćĺAsk: "How many email accounts are you sending from?"
    30ÔćĺAsk: "Have those been warmed up, or are they fresh?"
    31Ôćĺ
    32ÔćĺSave:
    33Ôćĺ- `nurge config set sending.domain_type "<dedicated|primary>"`
    34Ôćĺ- `nurge config set sending.mailbox_count <number>`
    35Ôćĺ- `nurge config set sending.warmup_status "<warmed|fresh>"`
    36Ôćĺ
    37ÔćĺSet limits: Fresh = 5/day. Primary domain = warn about risk. Warmed + dedicated = 30/mailbox/day.
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ---
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ## Z2: Deliverability Check
    42Ôćĺ
    43ÔćĺRun `dig TXT <domain>` for SPF, DKIM, DMARC. Flag issues with fix instructions.
    44Ôćĺ
    45ÔćĺSave: `nurge config set sending.deliverability_checked true`
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ---
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ## Z3: Prospect Geography
    50Ôćĺ
    51ÔćĺAsk: "Where are your prospects mostly located?" (US / EU / Global / specific)
    52Ôćĺ
    53ÔćĺSave: `nurge config set prospects.geography "<region>"`
    54Ôćĺ
    55Ôćĺ---
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ## S1: Disqualifiers ÔÇö "Who should I NOT waste your time on?"
    58Ôćĺ
    59ÔćĺNurge proposes based on product + ICP signals from website (F2):
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ"Based on your product, I'd skip:
    62Ôćĺ- Companies under [X] people (no budget)
    63Ôćĺ- Companies over [Y] (enterprise sales cycle you can't support)
    64Ôćĺ- [Industry] companies (not your market)
    65Ôćĺ- Companies already using [product category] (unless switching)
    66Ôćĺ
    67ÔćĺWhat else should I skip?"
    68Ôćĺ
    69ÔćĺUser tweaks. Save: `nurge config set qualification.disqualifiers '<json array>'`
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ---
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ## S2: ICP ÔÇö "Who IS worth your time?"
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ**Zero maturity: propose from website + LinkedIn research.**
    76Ôćĺ
    77ÔćĺUse website data from F2 (product, customers mentioned, positioning).
    78ÔćĺUse LinkedIn: `nurge enrich search --keywords "<product category>"` to see what kinds of companies exist in this space.
    79Ôćĺ
    80Ôćĺ"Based on your website, you sell [product] to [market]. Companies worth emailing:
    81Ôćĺ- [Size] employees
    82Ôćĺ- [Industry/vertical]
    83Ôćĺ- [Stage: startup/growth/enterprise]
    84Ôćĺ- [Geography from Z3]
    85Ôćĺ
    86ÔćĺSound right? Let's start narrow -- one segment."
    87Ôćĺ
    88ÔćĺSave: `nurge config set icp.company_size "<range>"`, `icp.industry`, `icp.stage`, `icp.geography`
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ---
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ## S3: Personas + Buying Committee ÔÇö "Who do you talk to, and who else gets involved?"
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ**PART A: Primary personas**
    95Ôćĺ
    96ÔćĺNurge proposes 2-3 personas from website + product type:
    97Ôćĺ
    98Ôćĺ"Within those companies, your buyers are probably:
    99Ôćĺ1. [Title] -- [why they care about your product]
   100Ôćĺ2. [Title] -- [why they care]
   101Ôćĺ3. [Title] -- [why they care]
   102Ôćĺ
   103ÔćĺRight? Anyone missing?"
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ**PART B: Buying committee**
   106Ôćĺ
   107ÔćĺZero maturity has no email CC patterns to analyze. Ask directly:
   108Ôćĺ
   109Ôćĺ"When someone buys your product, who else gets involved?
   110Ôćĺa) Just the person I email decides alone
   111Ôćĺb) A technical person evaluates
   112Ôćĺc) Their boss approves
   113Ôćĺd) Procurement gets involved
   114Ôćĺe) Not sure yet -- I'll learn as we go"
   115Ôćĺ
   116ÔćĺBased on answer, set committee pattern:
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ- (a) Ôćĺ single_decision_maker
   119Ôćĺ- (b) Ôćĺ champion + evaluator
   120Ôćĺ- (c) Ôćĺ champion + approver
   121Ôćĺ- (d) Ôćĺ champion + evaluator + approver + procurement
   122Ôćĺ- (e) Ôćĺ champion_only (update later from email CC patterns)
   123Ôćĺ
   124ÔćĺSave: `nurge config set personas '<json array with committee_role per persona>'`
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ---
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ## S4: Transformation Models ÔÇö "What makes each buyer care?"
   129Ôćĺ
   130Ôćĺ**For EACH persona (2-3 max), Nurge does external research:**
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ1. LinkedIn research:
   133Ôćĺ   `nurge enrich search --keywords "<persona title> <icp industry>"` -- find 20-30 real people
   134Ôćĺ   Look at their headlines, what they call themselves, what companies they're at.
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ2. Competitor/market research:
   137Ôćĺ   If G2 URL found in F2 links: WebFetch the G2 page for competitor reviews, complaints, praises.
   138Ôćĺ   If no G2 link: WebSearch "[product category] G2 reviews complaints"
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ3. Industry pain research:
   141Ôćĺ   WebSearch "[persona title] biggest challenge [industry] 2026"
   142Ôćĺ
   143Ôćĺ4. Propose transformation model:
   144Ôćĺ
   145Ôćĺ   "Here's what I found about [Persona] at your ICP companies:
   146Ôćĺ   - [Pain point 1 from research]
   147Ôćĺ   - [Pain point 2]
   148Ôćĺ   - [What tools they currently use]
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ   Your product changes:
   151Ôćĺ   - Current reality: '[what they do today without your product]'
   152Ôćĺ   - What changes: '[specific outcome with your product]'
   153Ôćĺ   - Who's affected: '[role on their team]'
   154Ôćĺ   - Where it fits: '[alongside/replaces what tool or process]'
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ   Hit close?"
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ   User validates or tweaks.
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ5. Store per persona:
   161Ôćĺ   `echo '<transformation json>' | nurge knowledge seed --atom transformation --scope "<persona title>"`
   162Ôćĺ
   163ÔćĺSave: `nurge config set framework.transformations_seeded true`
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ---
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ## S5: Timing Signals ÔÇö "How do I know they're ready RIGHT NOW?"
   168Ôćĺ
   169ÔćĺNurge proposes signals based on persona research from S4:
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ"Not every company matching your ICP is ready today. Here's what tells me the timing is right:
   172Ôćĺ
   173ÔćĺFor [Persona 1]:
   174Ôćĺ1. [Signal] -- [why it matters] -- I'll check [source]
   175Ôćĺ2. [Signal] -- [why it matters] -- I'll check [source]
   176Ôćĺ3. [Signal] -- [why it matters] -- I'll check [source]
   177Ôćĺ
   178ÔćĺFor [Persona 2]:
   179Ôćĺ1. [Signal] -- ...
   180Ôćĺ..."
   181Ôćĺ
   182ÔćĺCommon signals to propose (pick what's relevant):
   183Ôćĺ- **hiring_role**: Hiring people in the department your product serves
   184Ôćĺ- **team_turnover**: People leaving (from LinkedIn past_company search)
   185Ôćĺ- **recently_funded**: New money = new budget
   186Ôćĺ- **competitor_user**: Using a competitor (from job posts or G2)
   187Ôćĺ- **tech_stack_change**: Adopting new tech (from GitHub, job posts)
   188Ôćĺ- **small_gtm_team**: GTM headcount vs company size mismatch
   189Ôćĺ- **community_traction**: OSS stars or HN mentions without GTM to match
   190Ôćĺ- **leadership_change**: New VP/CTO/Head of (from LinkedIn or news)
   191Ôćĺ
   192ÔćĺFor each signal, define the check order (cheapest source first):
   193Ôćĺ
   194Ôćĺ```json
   195Ôćĺ{
   196Ôćĺ  "name": "hiring_role",
   197Ôćĺ  "check_order": ["website_careers", "linkedin_headlines"],
   198Ôćĺ  "stale_days": 14,
   199Ôćĺ  "applies_to": ["VP Sales"]
   200Ôćĺ}
   201Ôćĺ```
   202Ôćĺ
   203ÔćĺSave:
   204Ôćĺ`echo '<signals json>' | nurge knowledge seed --atom signals --scope global`
   205Ôćĺ`nurge config set framework.signals_seeded true`
   206Ôćĺ
   207Ôćĺ---
   208Ôćĺ
   209Ôćĺ## S6: Insight Priorities ÔÇö "What makes you impossible to ignore?"
   210Ôćĺ
   211ÔćĺNurge explains the insight hierarchy and lets user adjust per persona:
   212Ôćĺ
   213Ôćĺ"When I research a prospect, I look for something they don't expect you to know. Here's my priority:
   214Ôćĺ
   215Ôćĺ1. PEOPLE INSIGHT (strongest)
   216Ôćĺ   'I noticed 3 SDRs left your team last quarter'
   217Ôćĺ   Source: LinkedIn team data
   218Ôćĺ
   219Ôćĺ2. TOOL/STACK INSIGHT
   220Ôćĺ   'Your team uses [competitor] but your job posts mention [alternative]'
   221Ôćĺ   Source: GitHub + job posts
   222Ôćĺ
   223Ôćĺ3. COMPANY MOMENT INSIGHT
   224Ôćĺ   'You raised $X and pivoted to [new product]'
   225Ôćĺ   Source: funding data + website changes
   226Ôćĺ
   227Ôćĺ4. MARKET INSIGHT (weakest fallback)
   228Ôćĺ   '[Industry] teams spend 40% of time on [problem]'
   229Ôćĺ   Source: industry reports
   230Ôćĺ
   231ÔćĺI'll try #1 first, fall back down the list. Is this priority right for your buyers?"
   232Ôćĺ
   233ÔćĺUser may adjust per persona: "For VP Eng, tool insight is stronger than people insight."
   234Ôćĺ
   235ÔćĺSave:
   236Ôćĺ`echo '<insight_priority json>' | nurge knowledge seed --atom insight_priority --scope "<persona>"`
   237Ôćĺ`nurge config set framework.insights_seeded true`
   238Ôćĺ
   239Ôćĺ---
   240Ôćĺ
   241Ôćĺ## S7: Voice + Positioning
   242Ôćĺ
   243Ôćĺ**Positioning:** Already have it from F2 website scrape. Present a one-liner for cold emails:
   244Ôćĺ
   245Ôćĺ"[Name], for cold emails I'd lead with: '[positioning]'. Sound right?"
   246Ôćĺ
   247ÔćĺIterate until it clicks. Under 15 words, benefit-focused.
   248Ôćĺ
   249ÔćĺSave: `nurge config set product.positioning "<one-liner>"`
   250Ôćĺ
   251Ôćĺ**Social proof:** Check website data from F2. Present any proof points found. Ask to add/remove.
   252Ôćĺ
   253ÔćĺSave: `nurge config set product.social_proof '<json or none>'`
   254Ôćĺ
   255Ôćĺ**Voice:** Zero maturity has no emails to extract from. Ask:
   256Ôćĺ
   257Ôćĺ"How do you usually write to prospects?
   258Ôćĺa) Casual, like texting a friend
   259Ôćĺb) Professional but warm
   260Ôćĺc) Direct, no fluff
   261Ôćĺd) Show me an example you like"
   262Ôćĺ
   263ÔćĺIf (d): user provides an example, extract tone from it.
   264Ôćĺ
   265ÔćĺSeed baseline voice:
   266Ôćĺ`echo '<tone json>' | nurge knowledge seed --atom tone --scope global`
   267Ôćĺ
   268ÔćĺSave: `nurge config set framework.voice_seeded true`
   269Ôćĺ
   270Ôćĺ---
   271Ôćĺ
   272Ôćĺ## S8: KPIs & Goals
   273Ôćĺ
   274ÔćĺAsk: "What's your main outbound goal?" (Book meetings / Build pipeline / Validate PMF)
   275Ôćĺ
   276ÔćĺSuggest conservative KPIs based on warmup status from Z1:
   277Ôćĺ- Fresh: 5 emails/day, 1-2 replies/week, 1 meeting/week
   278Ôćĺ- Warmed: 20-30/day, 5-10% reply rate, 2-3 meetings/week
   279Ôćĺ
   280ÔćĺSave: `nurge config set kpi.goal`, `kpi.daily_emails`, `kpi.weekly_meetings`, `kpi.reply_rate_target`
   281Ôćĺ
   282Ôćĺ---
   283Ôćĺ
   284Ôćĺ## S9: Seed Framework + Install Skills
   285Ôćĺ
   286Ôćĺ**Step A: Seed the prospect intelligence framework atom.**
   287Ôćĺ
   288ÔćĺCombine everything from S1-S7 into one framework:
   289Ôćĺ
   290Ôćĺ```bash
   291Ôćĺecho '{
   292Ôćĺ  "qualification": {
   293Ôćĺ    "icp": { ... from S2 ... },
   294Ôćĺ    "disqualifiers": [ ... from S1 ... ],
   295Ôćĺ    "must_have_signal": true
   296Ôćĺ  },
   297Ôćĺ  "buying_committee": { ... from S3 ... },
   298Ôćĺ  "personas": {

   308Ôćĺ}' | nurge knowledge seed --atom prospect_intelligence_framework --scope global

   311Ôćĺ**Step B: Seed remaining knowledge atoms** (hooks, positioning, compliance, structure):

   333Ôćĺ## S10: Live Test ÔÇö Full Framework Execution
   334Ôćĺ
   335ÔćĺDon't announce "this is a demo." Just do it.
   336Ôćĺ
   337Ôćĺ1. Brief recap (2-3 lines): "[Name], you're set up: [product], targeting [ICP], reaching [personas]."

   131Ôćĺfunc ListSendAttempts(d *sql.DB, limit int, status string) ([]SendAttempt, error) {
   132Ôćĺ	query := `
   133Ôćĺ		SELECT s.id, s.mailbox_id, m.email, s.enrollment_id, s.task_id, s.contact_email, s.send_type,
   134Ôćĺ			s.status, s.subject, s.error, s.message_id, s.provider_id, s.attempted_at
   135Ôćĺ		FROM send_attempts s
   136Ôćĺ		JOIN mailboxes m ON m.id = s.mailbox_id`
   137Ôćĺ	var args []any
   138Ôćĺ	if status != "" {
   139Ôćĺ		query += ` WHERE s.status = ?`
   140Ôćĺ		args = append(args, status)
   141Ôćĺ	}

   146Ôćĺ
   147Ôćĺ	rows, err := d.Query(query, args...)
   148Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   149Ôćĺ		return nil, err
   150Ôćĺ	}
   151Ôćĺ	defer rows.Close()
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ	var attempts []SendAttempt
   154Ôćĺ	for rows.Next() {
   155Ôćĺ		var item SendAttempt
   156Ôćĺ		if err := rows.Scan(

   158Ôćĺ			&item.SendType, &item.Status, &item.Subject, &item.Error, &item.MessageID, &item.ProviderID, &item.AttemptedAt,
   159Ôćĺ		); err != nil {
   160Ôćĺ			return nil, err
   161Ôćĺ		}
   162Ôćĺ		attempts = append(attempts, item)

   164Ôćĺ	return attempts, rows.Err()

    40Ôćĺ// ErrTokenInvalid is returned when the server confirms the token is invalid.

    44Ôćĺ// ValidateToken checks a token against the Convex backend.
    45Ôćĺ// Returns ErrTokenInvalid if server says token is invalid.

    47Ôćĺfunc (c *ConvexClient) ValidateToken(token string) (*User, error) {
    48Ôćĺ	body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]string{"token": token})
    49Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
    50Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshaling request: %w", err)
    51Ôćĺ	}
    52Ôćĺ	resp, err := c.httpClient.Post(
    53Ôćĺ		c.baseURL+"/api/validate-token",
    54Ôćĺ		"application/json",
    55Ôćĺ		bytes.NewReader(body),
    56Ôćĺ	)
    57Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
    58Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("calling validate-token: %w", err)
    59Ôćĺ	}
    60Ôćĺ	defer resp.Body.Close()
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ	data, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
    63Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
    64Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading response: %w", err)
    65Ôćĺ	}
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ	var result validateResponse
    68Ôćĺ	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &result); err != nil {
    69Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parsing response: %w", err)
    70Ôćĺ	}
    71Ôćĺ
    72Ôćĺ	if !result.Valid {
    73Ôćĺ		return nil, ErrTokenInvalid
    74Ôćĺ	}
    75Ôćĺ	return result.User, nil
    76Ôćĺ}

Perfect. Now I have comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the final report:

## COMPREHENSIVE REPORT: ALL EMAIL SENDING TRIGGERS IN NURGE

Based on thorough code analysis, here are **every way a user can trigger outbound email sending** in this codebase:

---

### 1. **MANUAL DIRECT SEND** (Explicit user action)
**Entry point:** `nurge send` command (`/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/send.go`)

**Flags:**
- `--to <email>` (required)
- `--subject <text>` (required)
- `--body <text>` (required)
- `--cc <email>` (optional)
- `--sequence <name>` (optional - enrolls in multi-step sequence)
- `--campaign <name>` (optional - tags campaign)
- `--contact-name <name>` (optional - stores contact name)
- `--task-id <id>` (optional - marks follow-up task as sent, advances sequence)
- `--send-type <type>` (optional - classifies as `first_touch`, `follow_up`, `reply`, or `manual`)

**Flow:**
- Builds `SendEmailRequest` payload
- Calls Convex `/api/send-email` endpoint via HTTP (auth required)
- Response can be synchronous (sent immediately) or queued
- If `--sequence` provided: contacts Convex to enroll contact in multi-step sequence with future steps
- Returns message ID or task ID

**From within skills:** All skills can invoke this via CLI:
- `nurge-outbound`: `nurge send --to <email> --subject <s> --body <b> [--sequence <name>]`
- `nurge-followup`: `nurge send --to <email> --subject <s> --body <b> --task-id <id>`
- `nurge-book`: `nurge send` (implied for meeting confirmations)
- `nurge-replies`: `nurge send` (implied for reply handling)

---

### 2. **SEQUENCE ENROLLMENT & FIRST-TOUCH SEND**
**Flow trigger:** User sends initial email with `--sequence` flag

When `--sequence <template>` is specified in `nurge send`:
1. Send command fetches template definition from DB
2. Extracts all steps from template (step 2, 3, etc.)
3. Sends initial email immediately (step 1)
4. Convex backend creates sequence enrollment record
5. Each subsequent step generates a follow-up task with scheduled due date

**Built-in templates available:**
- `default-3-touch` (steps at +0h, +72h, +96h)
- `gentle-2-touch` (steps at +0h, +120h)
- `test-3-touch-1m` (steps at +0h, +1m, +1m for testing)
- Custom templates via `nurge sequence templates add --file <json>`

**Location:** `internal/sequence/sequence.go` lines 278-300

---

### 3. **AUTOMATED SEQUENCE TASK DISPATCH** (Unattended background sending)
**Entry point:** `nurge dispatcher run --execute` (`/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/dispatcher.go`)

**What it does:**
- Lists all due follow-up tasks (from sequence enrollments)
- Filters by mailbox policy (daily caps, send windows, health status)
- Checks suppression rules, bounce history, reply state
- **Renders email content** (template variables substituted)
- Sends via Gmail API if task is "ready"
- Records send attempts + mailbox usage

**Flags:**
- `--execute` (actually send; without this, only lists what would be sent)
- `--sync-replies true|false` (sync Gmail replies before evaluating queue)
- `--transport-email <address>` (override transport mailbox)
- `--limit N` (max tasks to process)

**Output:** JSON showing status of each task attempted (sent/failed)

**Function:** `executeDispatch()` (line 242-355) - sends via `email.Send(srv, fromAddress, options)` which calls Gmail API

---

### 4. **UNATTENDED AUTOMATED DISPATCH VIA CRON/LAUNCHD**
**Setup command:** `nurge dispatcher install`

**What it generates:**
1. Shell wrapper script: `.nurge/bin/dispatcher-run.sh`
2. launchd plist file: `.nurge/automation/dispatcher.plist`
3. Cron expression (if interval is divisible by 60)

**How it works:**
- macOS: launchd runs wrapper on `StartInterval` (seconds)
- Linux/other: User copies cron line: `*/5 * * * * /path/to/dispatcher-run.sh`
- Wrapper always executes: `nurge dispatcher run --execute [options]`
- Output appended to `.nurge/logs/dispatcher.log`
- Runs in background; machine must stay on, auth must stay valid

**Flags from install:**
- `--interval-minutes N` (frequency, default 5)
- `--sync-replies true|false` (whether to sync Gmail before each run)
- `--transport-email <address>` (pin to specific mailbox)

**Location:** `internal/automation/wrapper.go` - generates script and plist templates

---

### 5. **FOLLOW-UP TASKS FROM SEQUENCE ADVANCEMENT**
**Triggered when:** Task is marked as sent and sequence has more steps

**Flow:**
1. After a task sends (via dispatcher or manual send + `--task-id`)
2. `AdvanceTaskAfterSend()` fires (`internal/sequence/sequence.go` line 601)
3. If not on final step: creates next follow-up task with:
   - Due date = (send time) + (step delay)
   - Rendered subject/body from template
   - Status = `open` (pending)
4. Next task appears in `nurge tasks list` when due
5. When dispatcher runs, it picks up task and sends it

**Entry points for advancement:**
- `nurge dispatcher run --execute` (automatically after send)
- `nurge send --task-id <id>` (marks task as sent, triggers advancement)

---

### 6. **TASK-BASED FOLLOW-UP SENDING**
**Entry point:** `nurge tasks list`, `nurge tasks snooze`, `nurge tasks cancel`

**How it relates to sending:**
- Lists persisted follow-up tasks from active sequences OR infers from email history
- User can snooze (`--days N`) to delay sending
- User can cancel (`--reason <text>`) to stop sending
- When due, dispatcher picks them up automatically
- Or user manually drafts + sends via `nurge send --task-id <id>`

**Location:** `cmd/tasks.go`

---

### 7. **SEQUENCE ENROLLMENT MANAGEMENT**
**Commands:** `nurge sequence list`, `nurge sequence pause <id>`, `nurge sequence resume <id>`

**Pausing/resuming:** Enrollment status controls whether dispatcher includes tasks
- `active` Ôćĺ dispatcher will send tasks
- `paused` Ôćĺ dispatcher skips tasks
- `completed` Ôćĺ no more tasks (final step sent)
- `stopped` Ôćĺ enrollment canceled

**Location:** `cmd/sequence.go`

---

### 8. **CAMPAIGN-BASED SENDING BATCHES**
**Commands:** `nurge campaigns create <name>`, `nurge campaigns pause`, `nurge campaigns resume`

**What it is:** Metadata layer for grouping sequence enrollments
- Associates campaign name with sequence template + mailbox
- Allows batch pause/resume of all enrollments in campaign
- Used by dispatcher to track sends per campaign

**Flow:**
1. Create campaign: `nurge campaigns create "Q1_Outreach" --sequence default-3-touch --mailbox sales@company.com`
2. When user sends with `--sequence default-3-touch --campaign Q1_Outreach`, enrollment tagged
3. Campaign pause stops dispatcher from sending all enrollments in that campaign

**Location:** `cmd/campaigns.go`

---

### 9. **SKILL-DRIVEN SENDS (CLI INVOCATION)**
**Templates:**

All 17 thin skills can reference sending via CLI:

| Skill | Send Commands |
|-------|---------------|
| `nurge-outbound` | `nurge send --to <email> --subject <s> --body <b> [--sequence <name>]` |
| `nurge-followup` | `nurge send --to <email> --subject <s> --body <b> --task-id <id>` |
| `nurge-sequence` | `nurge send` (implied for sequence workflow) |
| `nurge-replies` | `nurge send` (implied for reply handling) |
| `nurge-book` | `nurge send` (implied for meeting booking) |

**Execution via Claude Code:**
- User invokes skill (e.g., `/nurge:outbound`)
- Skill runs `nurge context assemble --task outbound --prospect <email> --format markdown`
- Assembly engine outputs instructions
- User drafts email, skill suggests `nurge send` command
- User approves, command executes

**Location:** `internal/assembly/templates.go` lines 34-88, 156-170

---

### 10. **PHASE 2 ZERO: S10 LIVE TEST**
**Documentation:** `internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-zero.md` lines 333-376

**What happens at S10 (step 10 of zero-maturity setup):**
1. Full prospect intelligence framework assembly runs
2. Single test email drafted and presented to user
3. User decides: "Want to send it, or should I adjust?"
4. If yes Ôćĺ `nurge send` executed manually
5. Email sent immediately, prospect enrolled in sequence if `--sequence` specified
6. Sets `setup_completed true` flag

**Commands used in test:**
- `nurge enrich company <domain>`
- `nurge enrich person <email>`
- `nurge research save --prospect <email> --stdin`
- `nurge send --to <email> --subject <s> --body <b>`

---

### 11. **SEND ATTEMPT RECORDING & INSPECTION**
**Commands:** `nurge sends list --status <status> --limit N --format json`

**What it shows:** Historical record of all send attempts
- Status: `sent`, `failed`, `queued`
- Send type: `first_touch`, `follow_up`, `reply`, `manual`
- Mailbox, contact, subject, message ID, error (if failed)

**Does not send emails, but tracks them.**

**Location:** `cmd/sends.go`

---

### 12. **REPLY HANDLING & AUTO-RESPONSES**
**Commands:** `nurge replies queue`, `nurge replies done <id>`

**How it relates to sending:**
- `replies queue` shows incoming replies needing response
- User can draft reply via `nurge-replies` skill
- Skill suggests `nurge send` to send the reply
- Classified as `send_type: "reply"` for rate-limiting/health tracking

**Location:** `cmd/replies.go`

---

### 13. **CONTACT TIERING & BATCH QUALIFICATION**
**Commands:** `nurge tier --batch --limit N`, `nurge tier --prospect <email>`

**How it relates to sending:**
- Scores unscored contacts by ICP fit + signals
- Does NOT send emails itself
- But feeds into qualification framework
- Can inform decisions about which prospects to enroll in sequences

**Location:** `cmd/tier.go`

---

### 14. **CONVEX BACKEND SENDING**
**Cloud endpoint:** `POST /api/send-email` (called by `nurge send` command)

**What Convex does:**
- Receives `SendEmailRequest` with email + optional sequence enrollment details
- Can return `queued: true` (email will send from cloud)
- Can return `queued: false` (sent immediately)
- Creates sequence enrollment record if `--sequence` provided
- Manages cloud-side queue + rate-limiting
- Returns `enrollmentId` for future reference

**Request structure:** `internal/account/convex.go` lines 118-147:
```go
type SendEmailRequest struct {
    To       string           // recipient
    Cc       string           // optional CC
    Subject  string
    Body     string           // plain text
    SendType string           // "first_touch" | "follow_up" | "reply" | "manual"
    Sync     bool             // CLI sends are always true
    TaskID   string           // link to local task
    Sequence *SequenceRequest // if enrolling in sequence
}
```

---

### 15. **GMAIL API DIRECT SENDING**
**Low-level:** `internal/email/send.go` - `Send(srv *gmail.Service, from string, opts SendOptions)`

**When called:**
- By dispatcher.run --execute (for follow-up tasks)
- Not called directly by `nurge send` (goes through Convex instead)

**Builds raw SMTP message:** `From:`, `To:`, `Cc:`, `Subject:`, `Body:` headers

---

### 16. **SESSION SYNC PULL - REMOTE SENDS**
**When it happens:** Session start hook (`nurge hook session-start`)

**Flow:**
1. At session start, attempts to sync with Convex backend
2. Pulls all executions since last version (including sends completed remotely)
3. Distills memory: "X follow-ups sent remotely", "Y send failures", etc.
4. Reports in banner: "While you were away: 5 follow-ups sent | 2 replies need attention"

**Shows execution delta but doesn't directly send emails.**

**Location:** `cmd/hook.go` lines 534-586

---

### 17. **EMAIL FREQUENCY/RATE LIMITING**
**How sends are throttled (NOT a sending trigger, but a gate):**

Checked before dispatcher sends via `CanSend()` (`internal/sequence/sequence.go` line 417):
- Mailbox daily caps: `first_touch_daily_cap`, `follow_up_daily_cap`, `reply_daily_cap`
- Send window: `SendWindowStartHour` to `SendWindowEndHour` (timezone-aware)
- Minimum gap: `MinGapMinutes` between sends from same mailbox
- Recent bounce: blocks first-touches if >8% bounce rate in last 10 sends
- Suppression: blocks if contact is suppressed

**Location:** `internal/sequence/sequence.go` lines 410-470

---

## SUMMARY TABLE

| Method | User Action | Automation | Cloud | Source File |
|--------|-------------|-----------|-------|------------|
| **Manual send** | `nurge send --to ... --subject ... --body ...` | - | Convex HTTP | cmd/send.go |
| **Sequence send** | `nurge send --sequence <template>` | Initial + future steps | Convex + Dispatcher | cmd/send.go + dispatcher.go |
| **Dispatcher auto-send** | Setup once: `nurge dispatcher install` | Cron/launchd every N min | Local Gmail API | cmd/dispatcher.go |
| **Task-driven send** | `nurge send --task-id <id>` | Dispatcher picks up next task | Local Gmail API | cmd/send.go |
| **Skill-driven send** | `/nurge:outbound`, `/nurge:followup`, etc. | User approves then `nurge send` | Convex HTTP | assembly/templates.go |
| **Campaign batch** | `nurge campaigns create` then send with `--campaign` | Pause/resume all at once | Local DB | cmd/campaigns.go |
| **Reply handling** | User sees reply, drafts response via skill | `nurge send` + `send_type: reply` | Convex HTTP | cmd/replies.go |
| **Phase 2 S10 test** | User gives company name at setup | One-off demo send | Convex HTTP | phase-2-zero.md |

---

## COMPLETE EMAIL SENDING PATHS

**Path 1: Manual one-off send**
```
User Ôćĺ nurge send --to X --subject Y --body Z Ôćĺ Convex /api/send-email Ôćĺ Gmail or queued
```

**Path 2: Sequence enrollment with automated follow-ups**
```
User Ôćĺ nurge send --to X --subject Y --body Z --sequence "default-3-touch"
  Ôćĺ Convex creates enrollment + task 2,3
  Ôćĺ (Later) nurge dispatcher run --execute
    Ôćĺ Renders task 2, checks mailbox policy, sends via Gmail
    Ôćĺ AdvanceTaskAfterSend() Ôćĺ Creates task 3
  Ôćĺ (Later) Dispatcher sends task 3
```

**Path 3: Unattended background automation**
```
User setup: nurge dispatcher install --interval-minutes 5
  Ôćĺ Creates .nurge/bin/dispatcher-run.sh + launchd/cron wrapper
  Ôćĺ macOS launchd OR Linux cron runs: nurge dispatcher run --execute
    Ôćĺ For each due task: render, check policy, send
    Ôćĺ Update task status, create next task
    Ôćĺ (Repeats every 5 minutes)
```

**Path 4: Skill-driven with approval gate**
```
User: /nurge:outbound
  Ôćĺ nurge context assemble --task outbound --prospect X@Y.com
  Ôćĺ Skill shows: "Here's your email (85 words)"
  Ôćĺ User: "Send it"
  Ôćĺ nurge send --to X@Y.com --subject <suggested> --body <drafted>
  Ôćĺ Same as Path 1 or 2
```

**Path 5: Follow-up to reply**
```
Prospect replies to initial email
  Ôćĺ (Later) nurge dispatcher run / session start pulls replies
  Ôćĺ User: /nurge:replies Ôćĺ nurge replies queue
  Ôćĺ User drafts response via skill
  Ôćĺ nurge send --to X@Y.com --subject <reply> --body <drafted>
  Ôćĺ send_type recorded as "reply", not "first_touch"
```

---

## KEY FILES TO REFERENCE

- **Send command:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/send.go` (lines 27-170)
- **Dispatcher:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/dispatcher.go` (lines 49-240)
- **Sequence logic:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/sequence/sequence.go` (lines 487-780)

- **Gmail send:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/email/send.go` (lines 25-54)
- **Skill templates:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/assembly/templates.go` (lines 14-131)
- **Automation:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/automation/wrapper.go` (entire file)
- **Setup/framework:** `/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-zero.md` (S10 section)

3. Sends initial email immediately (step 1)
4. Convex backend creates sequence enrollment record
5. Each subsequent step generates a follow-up task with scheduled due date

**Built-in templates available:**
- `default-3-touch` (steps at +0h, +72h, +96h)
- `gentle-2-touch` (steps at +0h, +120h)
- `test-3-touch-1m` (steps at +0h, +1m, +1m for testing)
- Custom templates via `nurge sequence templates add --file <json>`

**Location:** `internal/sequence/sequence.go` lines 278-300

---

### 3. **AUTOMATED SEQUENCE TASK DISPATCH** (Unattended background sending)
**Entry point:** `nurge dispatcher run --execute` (`/Users/rejoesudarsan/Projects/nurgesh/cmd/dispatcher.go`)

**What it does:**
- Lists all due follow-up tasks (from sequence enrollments)
- Filters by mailbox policy (daily caps, send windows, health status)
- Checks suppression rules, bounce history, reply state
- **Renders email content** (template variables substituted)
- Sends via Gmail API if task is "ready"
- Records send attempts + mailbox usage

**Flags:**
- `--execute` (actually send; without this, only lists what would be sent)
- `--sync-replies true|false` (sync Gmail replies before evaluating queue)
- `--transport-email <address>` (override transport mailbox)
- `--limit N` (max tasks to process)

**Output:** JSON showing status of each task attempted (sent/failed)

**Function:** `executeDispatch()` (line 242-355) - sends via `email.Send(srv, fromAddress, options)` which calls Gmail API

---

### 4. **UNATTENDED AUTOMATED DISPATCH VIA CRON/LAUNCHD**
**Setup command:** `nurge dispatcher install`

**What it generates:**
1. Shell wrapper script: `.nurge/bin/dispatcher-run.sh`
2. launchd plist file: `.nurge/automation/dispatcher.plist`
3. Cron expression (if interval is divisible by 60)

**How it works:**
- macOS: launchd runs wrapper on `StartInterval` (seconds)
- Linux/other: User copies cron line: `*/5 * * * * /path/to/dispatcher-run.sh`
- Wrapper always executes: `nurge dispatcher run --execute [options]`
- Output appended to `.nurge/logs/dispatcher.log`
- Runs in background; machine must stay on, auth must stay valid

**Flags from install:**
- `--interval-minutes N` (frequency, default 5)
- `--sync-replies true|false` (whether to sync Gmail before each run)
- `--transport-email <address>` (pin to specific mailbox)

**Location:** `internal/automation/wrapper.go` - generates script and plist templates

---

### 5. **FOLLOW-UP TASKS FROM SEQUENCE ADVANCEMENT**
**Triggered when:** Task is marked as sent and sequence has more steps

**Flow:**
1. After a task sends (via dispatcher or manual send + `--task-id`)
2. `AdvanceTaskAfterSend()` fires (`internal/sequence/sequence.go` line 601)
3. If not on final step: creates next follow-up task with:
   - Due date = (send time) + (step delay)
   - Rendered subject/body from template
   - Status = `open` (pending)
4. Next task appears in `nurge tasks list` when due
5. When dispatcher runs, it picks up task and sends it

**Entry points for advancement:**
- `nurge dispatcher run --execute` (automatically after send)
- `nurge send --task-id <id>` (marks task as sent, triggers advancement)

---

### 6. **TASK-BASED FOLLOW-UP SENDING**
**Entry point:** `nurge tasks list`, `nurge tasks snooze`, `nurge tasks cancel`

**How it relates to sending:**
- Lists persisted follow-up tasks from active sequences OR infers from email history
- User can snooze (`--days N`) to delay sending
- User can cancel (`--reason <text>`) to stop sending
- When due, dispatcher picks them up automatically
- Or user manually drafts + sends via `nurge send --task-id <id>`

**Location:** `cmd/tasks.go`

---

### 7. **SEQUENCE ENROLLMENT MANAGEMENT**
**Commands:** `nurge sequence list`, `nurge sequence pause <id>`, `nurge sequence resume <id>`

**Pausing/resuming:** Enrollment status controls whether dispatcher includes tasks
- `active` Ôćĺ dispatcher will send tasks
- `paused` Ôćĺ dispatcher skips tasks
- `completed` Ôćĺ no more tasks (final step sent)
- `stopped` Ôćĺ enrollment canceled

**Location:** `cmd/sequence.go`

---

### 8. **CAMPAIGN-BASED SENDING BATCHES**
**Commands:** `nurge campaigns create <name>`, `nurge campaigns pause`, `nurge campaigns resume`

**What it is:** Metadata layer for grouping sequence enrollments
- Associates campaign name with sequence template + mailbox
- Allows batch pause/resume of all enrollments in campaign
- Used by dispatcher to track sends per campaign

**Flow:**
1. Create campaign: `nurge campaigns create "Q1_Outreach" --sequence default-3-touch --mailbox sales@company.com`
2. When user sends with `--sequence default-3-touch --campaign Q1_Outreach`, enrollment tagged
3. Campaign pause stops dispatcher from sending all enrollments in that campaign

**Location:** `cmd/campaigns.go`

---

### 9. **SKILL-DRIVEN SENDS (CLI INVOCATION)**
**Templates:**

All 17 thin skills can reference sending via CLI:

| Skill | Send Commands |
|-------|---------------|
| `nurge-outbound` | `nurge send --to <email> --subject <s> --body <b> [--sequence <name>]` |
| `nurge-followup` | `nurge send --to <email> --subject <s> --body <b> --task-id <id>` |
| `nurge-sequence` | `nurge send` (implied for sequence workflow) |
| `nurge-replies` | `nurge send` (implied for reply handling) |
| `nurge-book` | `nurge send` (implied for meeting booking) |

**Execution via Claude Code:**
- User invokes skill (e.g., `/nurge:outbound`)
- Skill runs `nurge context assemble --task outbound --prospect <email> --format markdown`
- Assembly engine outputs instructions
- User drafts email, skill suggests `nurge send` command
- User approves, command executes

**Location:** `internal/assembly/templates.go` lines 34-88, 156-170

---

### 10. **PHASE 2 ZERO: S10 LIVE TEST**
**Documentation:** `internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-zero.md` lines 333-376

**What happens at S10 (step 10 of zero-maturity setup):**
1. Full prospect intelligence framework assembly runs
2. Single test email drafted and presented to user
3. User decides: "Want to send it, or should I adjust?"
4. If yes Ôćĺ `nurge send` executed manually
5. Email sent immediately, prospect enrolled in sequence if `--sequence` specified
6. Sets `setup_completed true` flag

**Commands used in test:**
- `nurge enrich company <domain>`
- `nurge enrich person <email>`
- `nurge research save --prospect <email> --stdin`
- `nurge send --to <email> --subject <s> --body <b>`

---

### 11. **SEND ATTEMPT RECORDING & INSPECTION**
**Commands:** `nurge sends list --status <status> --limit N --format json`

**What it shows:** Historical record of all send attempts
- Status: `sent`, `failed`, `queued`
- Send type: `first_touch`, `follow_up`, `reply`, `manual`
- Mailbox, contact, subject, message ID, error (if failed)

**Does not send emails, but tracks them.**

**Location:** `cmd/sends.go`

---

### 12. **REPLY HANDLING & AUTO-RESPONSES**
**Commands:** `nurge replies queue`, `nurge replies done <id>`

**How it relates to sending:**
- `replies queue` shows incoming replies needing response
- User can draft reply via `nurge-replies` skill
- Skill suggests `nurge send` to send the reply
- Classified as `send_type: "reply"` for rate-limiting/health tracking

**Location:** `cmd/replies.go`

---

### 13. **CONTACT TIERING & BATCH QUALIFICATION**
**Commands:** `nurge tier --batch --limit N`, `nurge tier --prospect <email>`

**How it relates to sending:**
- Scores unscored contacts by ICP fit + signals
- Does NOT send emails itself
- But feeds into qualification framework
- Can inform decisions about which prospects to enroll in sequences

**Location:** `cmd/tier.go`

---

### 14. **CONVEX BACKEND SENDING**
**Cloud endpoint:** `POST /api/send-email` (called by `nurge send` command)

**What Convex does:**
- Receives `SendEmailRequest` with email + optional sequence enrollment details
- Can return `queued: true` (email will send from cloud)
- Can return `queued: false` (sent immediately)
- Creates sequence enrollment record if `--sequence` provided
- Manages cloud-side queue + rate-limiting
- Returns `enrollmentId` for future reference

**Request structure:** `internal/account/convex.go` lines 118-147:
```go
type SendEmailRequest struct {
    To       string           // recipient
    Cc       string           // optional CC
    Subject  string
    Body     string           // plain text
    SendType string           // "first_touch" | "follow_up" | "reply" | "manual"
    Sync     bool             // CLI sends are always true
    TaskID   string           // link to local task
    Sequence *SequenceRequest // if enrolling in sequence
}
```

---

### 15. **GMAIL API DIRECT SENDING**
**Low-level:** `internal/email/send.go` - `Send(srv *gmail.Service, from string, opts SendOptions)`

**When called:**
- By dispatcher.run --execute (for follow-up tasks)
- Not called directly by `nurge send` (goes through Convex instead)

**Builds raw SMTP message:** `From:`, `To:`, `Cc:`, `Subject:`, `Body:` headers

---

### 16. **SESSION SYNC PULL - REMOTE SENDS**
**When it happens:** Session start hook (`nurge hook session-start`)

**Flow:**
1. At session start, attempts to sync with Convex backend
2. Pulls all executions since last version (including sends completed remotely)
3. Distills memory: "X follow-ups sent remotely", "Y send failures", etc.
4. Reports in banner: "While you were away: 5 follow-ups sent | 2 replies need attention"

**Shows execution delta but doesn't directly send emails.**

**Location:** `cmd/hook.go` lines 534-586

---

### 17. **EMAIL FREQUENCY/RATE LIMITING**
**How sends are throttled (NOT a sending trigger, but a gate):**

Checked before dispatcher sends via `CanSend()` (`internal/sequence/sequence.go` line 417):
- Mailbox daily caps: `first_touch_daily_cap`, `follow_up_daily_cap`, `reply_daily_cap`
- Send window: `SendWindowStartHour` to `SendWindowEndHour` (timezone-aware)
- Minimum gap: `MinGapMinutes` between sends from same mailbox
- Recent bounce: blocks first-touches if >8% bounce rate in last 10 sends
- Suppression: blocks if contact is suppressed

**Location:** `internal/sequence/sequence.go` lines 410-470

---

## SUMMARY TABLE

| Method | User Action | Automation | Cloud | Source File |
|--------|-------------|-----------|-------|------------|
| **Manual send** | `nurge send --to ... --subject ... --body ...` | - | Convex HTTP | cmd/send.go |
| **Sequence send** | `nurge send --sequence <template>` | Initial + future steps | Convex + Dispatcher | cmd/send.go + dispatcher.go |
| **Dispatcher auto-send** | Setup once: `nurge dispatcher install` | Cron/launchd every N min | Local Gmail API | cmd/dispatcher.go |
| **Task-driven send** | `nurge send --task-id <id>` | Dispatcher picks up next task | Local Gmail API | cmd/send.go |
| **Skill-driven send** | `/nurge:outbound`, `/nurge:followup`, etc. | User approves then `nurge send` | Convex HTTP | assembly/templates.go |
| **Campaign batch** | `nurge campaigns create` then send with `--campaign` | Pause/resume all at once | Local DB | cmd/campaigns.go |
| **Reply handling** | User sees reply, drafts response via skill | `nurge send` + `send_type: reply` | Convex HTTP | cmd/replies.go |
| **Phase 2 S10 test** | User gives company name at setup | One-off demo send | Convex HTTP | phase-2-zero.md |

---

## COMPLETE EMAIL SENDING PATHS

**Path 1: Manual one-off send**
```
User Ôćĺ nurge send --to X --subject Y --body Z Ôćĺ Convex /api/send-email Ôćĺ Gmail or queued
```

**Path 2: Sequence enrollment with automated follow-ups**
```
User Ôćĺ nurge send --to X --subject Y --body Z --sequence "default-3-touch"
  Ôćĺ Convex creates enrollment + task 2,3
  Ôćĺ (Later) nurge dispatcher run --execute
    Ôćĺ Renders task 2, checks mailbox policy, sends via Gmail
    Ôćĺ AdvanceTaskAfterSend() Ôćĺ Creates task 3
  Ôćĺ (Later) Dispatcher sends task 3
```

**Path 3: Unattended background automation**
```
User setup: nurge dispatcher install --interval-minutes 5
  Ôćĺ Creates .nurge/bin/dispatcher-run.sh + launchd/cron wrapper
  Ôćĺ macOS launchd OR Linux cron runs: nurge dispatcher run --execute
    Ôćĺ For each due task: render, check policy, send
    Ôćĺ Update task status, create next task
    Ôćĺ (Repeats every 5 minutes)
```

**Path 4: Skill-driven with approval gate**
```
User: /nurge:outbound
  Ôćĺ nurge context assemble --task outbound --prospect X@Y.com
  Ôćĺ Skill shows: "Here's your email (85 words)"
  Ôćĺ User: "Send it"
  Ôćĺ nurge send --to X@Y.com --subject <suggested> --body <drafted>
  Ôćĺ Same as Path 1 or 2
```

**Path 5: Follow-up to reply**
```
Prospect replies to initial email
  Ôćĺ (Later) nurge dispatcher run / session start pulls replies
  Ôćĺ User: /nurge:replies Ôćĺ nurge replies queue
  Ôćĺ User drafts response via skill
  Ôćĺ nurge send --to X@Y.com --subject <reply> --body <drafted>
  Ôćĺ send_type recorded as "reply", not "first_touch"
```

Here are all the ways outbound gets sent, from the actual code:

---

## 5 Sending Paths

### Path 1: One-off Manual Send
```
User drafts email (or Claude drafts via /nurge:outbound)
  Ôćĺ nurge send --to X --subject Y --body Z
  Ôćĺ Convex /api/send-email Ôćĺ Gmail
```
Single email, no follow-ups. User approves, it goes.

### Path 2: Sequence Enrollment (Manual + Automated Follow-ups)
```
nurge send --to X --subject Y --body Z --sequence "default-3-touch" --campaign "Q1"
  Ôćĺ Step 1 sends immediately via Convex
  Ôćĺ Steps 2, 3 become scheduled tasks (e.g. +72h, +96h)
  Ôćĺ Dispatcher picks them up when due
```
First touch is manual. Follow-ups are automated on a schedule.

Built-in templates: `default-3-touch`, `gentle-2-touch`, `test-3-touch-1m`. Custom via `nurge sequence templates add --file`.

### Path 3: Unattended Dispatcher (Background Automation)
```
nurge dispatcher install --interval-minutes 5 --sync-replies
  Ôćĺ Generates launchd plist (macOS) or cron line (Linux)
  Ôćĺ Every 5 min: nurge dispatcher run --execute
    Ôćĺ Syncs replies first (detects if prospect replied, auto-pauses)
    Ôćĺ Queries due tasks
    Ôćĺ Checks mailbox policy (daily cap, send window, warmup stage, bounce rate, min gap)
    Ôćĺ Sends via Gmail API
    Ôćĺ Advances sequence Ôćĺ creates next task
```
Fully hands-off. Once set up, follow-ups send on schedule without opening Claude.

### Path 4: Skill-Driven with Approval Gate
```
User: /nurge:outbound john@acme.com
  Ôćĺ Skill runs: nurge context assemble --task outbound --prospect john@acme.com
  Ôćĺ Assembly loads: tone atoms, positioning, persona, research dossier, learnings
  Ôćĺ Claude drafts email (50-90 words)
  Ôćĺ User reviews, iterates, approves
  Ôćĺ nurge send --to john@acme.com --subject "..." --body "..." [--sequence "3-touch"]
```
This is the primary flow. Claude does the thinking, user approves, then it's Path 1 or 2.

### Path 5: Task-Driven Follow-up (Manual Override)
```
nurge tasks list                    Ôćĺ see due follow-ups
nurge tasks snooze <id> --days 3    Ôćĺ delay one
User: /nurge:followup john@acme.com Ôćĺ Claude drafts follow-up
nurge send --to X --subject Y --body Z --task-id <id>
  Ôćĺ Marks task as sent
  Ôćĺ AdvanceTaskAfterSend() Ôćĺ creates next step
```
User intercepts a scheduled task, rewrites it via skill, then sends manually instead of letting the dispatcher auto-send.

---

## How They Connect

```
                    /nurge:outbound (skill)
                           Ôöé
                    Claude drafts email
                           Ôöé
                    User approves
                           Ôöé

    nurge send         nurge send          nurge send
    (one-off)     + --sequence "3-touch"   + --task-id
         Ôöé                 Ôöé                  Ôöé
         Ôöé          ÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔö┤ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ           Ôöé
         Ôöé          Ôöé             Ôöé           Ôöé
         Ôöé     Step 1 sends    Steps 2-3     Marks task sent
         Ôöé     immediately     become tasks   Advances sequence
         Ôöé                        Ôöé           Ôöé
         Ôöé                        Ôľ╝           Ôöé
         Ôöé              nurge dispatcher      Ôöé
         Ôöé              run --execute         Ôöé
         Ôöé                   Ôöé                Ôöé
         Ôöé            ÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔö┤ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ         Ôöé
         Ôöé            Ôöé             Ôöé         Ôöé
         Ôöé      Check policy    Send via     Ôöé
         Ôöé      (caps, window,  Gmail API    Ôöé
         Ôöé       warmup, bounce)     Ôöé        Ôöé
         Ôöé                    Advance to      Ôöé
         Ôöé                    next step       Ôöé
         Ôľ╝                        Ôľ╝           Ôľ╝

```

---

## Rate Limiting Gates (Applied to All Paths)

From `internal/sequence/sequence.go` `CanSend()`:

| Gate | What it checks |
|------|---------------|
| Daily cap | `first_touch_daily_cap`, `follow_up_daily_cap`, `reply_daily_cap` per mailbox |
| Send window | `SendWindowStartHour` to `SendWindowEndHour` (timezone-aware) |
| Min gap | `MinGapMinutes` between sends from same mailbox |
| Bounce rate | Blocks first-touches if >8% bounce in last 10 sends |
| Suppression | Blocks if contact is suppressed |
| Warmup stage | Week 1-2: 5/day, Week 3-4: 10-15/day, Week 5+: 20-30/day |

---

## Supporting Commands

| Command | Role |
|---------|------|
| `nurge sequence list [--status active]` | See all enrollments |
| `nurge sequence pause/resume <id>` | Control individual sequences |
| `nurge campaigns create/pause/resume` | Batch control groups of enrollments |
| `nurge tasks list` | See what's due |
| `nurge tasks snooze/cancel` | Delay or stop a follow-up |
| `nurge sends list --status sent` | Audit what was sent |
| `nurge replies queue` | See replies needing response |
| `nurge mailboxes status` | Check mailbox health + caps |

    16Ôćĺ// InputType classifies what the user provided as input.
    17Ôćĺtype InputType string

    19Ôćĺconst (
    20Ôćĺ	InputEmail           InputType = "email"
    21Ôćĺ	InputCSV             InputType = "csv"
    22Ôćĺ	InputLinkedInPerson  InputType = "linkedin_person"
    23Ôćĺ	InputLinkedInCompany InputType = "linkedin_company"
    24Ôćĺ	InputWebsite         InputType = "website"

    26Ôćĺ)

    28Ôćĺ// ResolveResult is the output of the resolve pipeline.
    29Ôćĺtype ResolveResult struct {

    34Ôćĺ	Summary   string             `json:"summary"`

    36Ôćĺ}

    38Ôćĺ// ResolvedContact represents a contact created or matched during resolve.

    45Ôćĺ	Status        string   `json:"status"` // created, updated, existing
    46Ôćĺ	MissingFields []string `json:"missing_fields,omitempty"`
    47Ôćĺ	IsNew         bool     `json:"is_new"`
    48Ôćĺ}
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ// ResolvedCompany represents a company extracted from a URL or domain.

    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ// SkippedEntry records an input that was not resolved.

    64Ôćĺ// DetectInputType determines what kind of input the user provided.
    65Ôćĺfunc DetectInputType(input string) InputType {
    66Ôćĺ	trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    67Ôćĺ	lower := strings.ToLower(trimmed)
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ	if strings.HasSuffix(lower, ".csv") {
    70Ôćĺ		return InputCSV
    71Ôćĺ	}
    72Ôćĺ	if strings.Contains(lower, "linkedin.com/in/") {
    73Ôćĺ		return InputLinkedInPerson
    74Ôćĺ	}
    75Ôćĺ	if strings.Contains(lower, "linkedin.com/company/") {
    76Ôćĺ		return InputLinkedInCompany
    77Ôćĺ	}
    78Ôćĺ	if strings.Contains(trimmed, "@") && looksLikeEmail(trimmed) {
    79Ôćĺ		return InputEmail
    80Ôćĺ	}
    81Ôćĺ	if looksLikeURL(trimmed) {
    82Ôćĺ		return InputWebsite
    83Ôćĺ	}
    84Ôćĺ	return InputNameCompany
    85Ôćĺ}
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ// Resolve is the main entry point. It detects input type and routes accordingly.
    88Ôćĺ// The company parameter is only used for InputNameCompany.
    89Ôćĺfunc Resolve(d *sql.DB, input string, company string) (*ResolveResult, error) {
    90Ôćĺ	if d == nil {
    91Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("database is required")
    92Ôćĺ	}
    93Ôćĺ	input = strings.TrimSpace(input)
    94Ôćĺ	if input == "" {
    95Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("input is required")
    96Ôćĺ	}
    97Ôćĺ
    98Ôćĺ	inputType := DetectInputType(input)
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ	switch inputType {
   101Ôćĺ	case InputEmail:
   102Ôćĺ		return resolveEmail(d, input)
   103Ôćĺ	case InputCSV:
   104Ôćĺ		return resolveCSV(d, input)
   105Ôćĺ	case InputLinkedInPerson:
   106Ôćĺ		return resolveLinkedInPerson(d, input)
   107Ôćĺ	case InputLinkedInCompany:
   108Ôćĺ		return resolveLinkedInCompany(input)
   109Ôćĺ	case InputWebsite:
   110Ôćĺ		return resolveWebsite(input)
   111Ôćĺ	case InputNameCompany:
   112Ôćĺ		return resolveNameCompany(d, input, company)
   113Ôćĺ	default:
   114Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported input type: %s", inputType)
   115Ôćĺ	}
   116Ôćĺ}

   118Ôćĺ// --- Email ---
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺfunc resolveEmail(d *sql.DB, input string) (*ResolveResult, error) {
   121Ôćĺ	email := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(input))
   122Ôćĺ	if !looksLikeEmail(email) {
   123Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid email address: %s", input)
   124Ôćĺ	}
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ	domain := domainFromEmail(email)
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ	// Check suppression
   129Ôćĺ	if suppressed, err := isSuppressed(d, email, domain); err != nil {
   130Ôćĺ		return nil, err
   131Ôćĺ	} else if suppressed {
   132Ôćĺ		return &ResolveResult{
   133Ôćĺ			InputType: InputEmail,
   134Ôćĺ			Skipped:   []SkippedEntry{{Input: email, Reason: "suppressed"}},
   135Ôćĺ			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("%s is suppressed", email),
   136Ôćĺ			Next:      "ready",
   137Ôćĺ		}, nil
   138Ôćĺ	}
   139Ôćĺ
   140Ôćĺ	// Check existing contact
   141Ôćĺ	existing, err := contactByEmail(d, email)
   142Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   143Ôćĺ		return nil, err
   144Ôćĺ	}
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ	if existing != nil {
   147Ôćĺ		missing := missingContactFields(existing)
   148Ôćĺ		next := "ready"
   149Ôćĺ		if len(missing) > 0 {
   150Ôćĺ			next = "enrich"
   151Ôćĺ		}
   152Ôćĺ		return &ResolveResult{
   153Ôćĺ			InputType: InputEmail,
   154Ôćĺ			Contacts: []ResolvedContact{{
   155Ôćĺ				ID:            existing.id,
   156Ôćĺ				Email:         existing.email,
   157Ôćĺ				Name:          existing.name,
   158Ôćĺ				Company:       existing.company,
   159Ôćĺ				Title:         existing.title,
   160Ôćĺ				Status:        "existing",
   161Ôćĺ				MissingFields: missing,
   162Ôćĺ				IsNew:         false,
   163Ôćĺ			}},
   164Ôćĺ			Summary: fmt.Sprintf("Found existing contact %s", email),
   165Ôćĺ			Next:    next,
   166Ôćĺ		}, nil
   167Ôćĺ	}
   168Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ	// Create new contact
   170Ôćĺ	id := newContactID()
   171Ôćĺ	now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ	_, err = d.Exec(`
   174Ôćĺ		INSERT INTO contacts (id, source, email, domain, resolve_source, last_activity_at, updated_at)
   175Ôćĺ		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   176Ôćĺ		id, "resolve", email, domain, string(InputEmail), now, now,
   177Ôćĺ	)
   178Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   179Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating contact: %w", err)
   180Ôćĺ	}
   181Ôćĺ
   182Ôćĺ	missing := []string{"name", "company", "title"}
   183Ôćĺ	return &ResolveResult{
   184Ôćĺ		InputType: InputEmail,
   185Ôćĺ		Contacts: []ResolvedContact{{
   186Ôćĺ			ID:            id,
   187Ôćĺ			Email:         email,
   188Ôćĺ			Name:          "",
   189Ôćĺ			Company:       "",
   190Ôćĺ			Title:         "",
   191Ôćĺ			Status:        "created",
   192Ôćĺ			MissingFields: missing,
   193Ôćĺ			IsNew:         true,
   194Ôćĺ		}},
   195Ôćĺ		Summary: fmt.Sprintf("Created contact for %s", email),
   196Ôćĺ		Next:    "enrich",
   197Ôćĺ	}, nil
   198Ôćĺ}

   200Ôćĺ// --- CSV ---
   201Ôćĺ
   202Ôćĺ// headerAliases maps canonical field names to common CSV header variations.
   203Ôćĺvar headerAliases = map[string][]string{
   204Ôćĺ	"email":    {"email", "email address", "e-mail", "mail"},
   205Ôćĺ	"name":     {"name", "full name", "contact name"},
   206Ôćĺ	"company":  {"company", "organization", "org", "company name"},
   207Ôćĺ	"title":    {"title", "job title", "role", "position"},
   208Ôćĺ	"phone":    {"phone", "phone number", "telephone"},
   209Ôćĺ	"linkedin": {"linkedin", "linkedin url", "linkedin_url", "profile"},
   210Ôćĺ}
   211Ôćĺ
   212Ôćĺfunc resolveCSV(d *sql.DB, path string) (*ResolveResult, error) {
   213Ôćĺ	f, err := os.Open(path)
   214Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   215Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening CSV: %w", err)
   216Ôćĺ	}
   217Ôćĺ	defer f.Close()
   218Ôćĺ
   219Ôćĺ	reader := csv.NewReader(f)
   220Ôćĺ	reader.TrimLeadingSpace = true
   221Ôćĺ	reader.FieldsPerRecord = -1 // Allow variable field counts
   222Ôćĺ
   223Ôćĺ	header, err := reader.Read()
   224Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   225Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading CSV header: %w", err)
   226Ôćĺ	}
   227Ôćĺ
   228Ôćĺ	colMap := buildColumnMap(header)
   229Ôćĺ
   230Ôćĺ	// We need at least an email column
   231Ôćĺ	if _, ok := colMap["email"]; !ok {
   232Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("CSV must include an email column (tried: %s)", strings.Join(headerAliases["email"], ", "))
   233Ôćĺ	}
   234Ôćĺ
   235Ôćĺ	result := &ResolveResult{
   236Ôćĺ		InputType: InputCSV,
   237Ôćĺ	}
   238Ôćĺ
   239Ôćĺ	seen := map[string]bool{}
   240Ôćĺ	var created, updated, existing int
   241Ôćĺ
   242Ôćĺ	for {
   243Ôćĺ		record, err := reader.Read()
   244Ôćĺ		if err == io.EOF {
   245Ôćĺ			break
   246Ôćĺ		}
   247Ôćĺ		if err != nil {
   248Ôćĺ			result.Skipped = append(result.Skipped, SkippedEntry{
   249Ôćĺ				Input:  fmt.Sprintf("row %d", created+updated+existing+len(result.Skipped)+1),
   250Ôćĺ				Reason: fmt.Sprintf("parse error: %s", err.Error()),
   251Ôćĺ			})
   252Ôćĺ			continue
   253Ôćĺ		}
   254Ôćĺ
   255Ôćĺ		email := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(colVal(record, colMap, "email")))
   256Ôćĺ		if !looksLikeEmail(email) {
   257Ôćĺ			result.Skipped = append(result.Skipped, SkippedEntry{Input: email, Reason: "invalid_email"})
   258Ôćĺ			continue
   259Ôćĺ		}
   260Ôćĺ		if seen[email] {
   261Ôćĺ			result.Skipped = append(result.Skipped, SkippedEntry{Input: email, Reason: "duplicate"})
   262Ôćĺ			continue
   263Ôćĺ		}
   264Ôćĺ		seen[email] = true
   265Ôćĺ
   266Ôćĺ		domain := domainFromEmail(email)
   267Ôćĺ		if suppressed, err := isSuppressed(d, email, domain); err != nil {
   268Ôćĺ			return nil, err
   269Ôćĺ		} else if suppressed {
   270Ôćĺ			result.Skipped = append(result.Skipped, SkippedEntry{Input: email, Reason: "suppressed"})
   271Ôćĺ			continue
   272Ôćĺ		}
   273Ôćĺ
   274Ôćĺ		name := strings.TrimSpace(colVal(record, colMap, "name"))
   275Ôćĺ		// Handle first_name + last_name combination
   276Ôćĺ		if name == "" {
   277Ôćĺ			first := strings.TrimSpace(colVal(record, colMap, "first_name"))
   278Ôćĺ			last := strings.TrimSpace(colVal(record, colMap, "last_name"))
   279Ôćĺ			name = strings.TrimSpace(first + " " + last)
   280Ôćĺ		}
   281Ôćĺ		company := strings.TrimSpace(colVal(record, colMap, "company"))
   282Ôćĺ		title := strings.TrimSpace(colVal(record, colMap, "title"))
   283Ôćĺ		phone := strings.TrimSpace(colVal(record, colMap, "phone"))
   284Ôćĺ		linkedin := strings.TrimSpace(colVal(record, colMap, "linkedin"))
   285Ôćĺ		if domain == "" {
   286Ôćĺ			domain = domainFromEmail(email)
   287Ôćĺ		}
   288Ôćĺ
   289Ôćĺ		existingContact, err := contactByEmail(d, email)
   290Ôćĺ		if err != nil {
   291Ôćĺ			return nil, err
   292Ôćĺ		}
   293Ôćĺ
   294Ôćĺ		if existingContact != nil {
   295Ôćĺ			// Update existing contact with non-empty fields
   296Ôćĺ			_, err = d.Exec(`
   297Ôćĺ				UPDATE contacts
   298Ôćĺ				SET name = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN name ELSE ? END,
   299Ôćĺ					domain = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN domain ELSE ? END,
   300Ôćĺ					company = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN company ELSE ? END,
   301Ôćĺ					title = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN title ELSE ? END,
   302Ôćĺ					phone = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN phone ELSE ? END,
   303Ôćĺ					linkedin_url = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN linkedin_url ELSE ? END,
   304Ôćĺ					resolve_source = CASE WHEN resolve_source = '' THEN ? ELSE resolve_source END,
   305Ôćĺ					last_activity_at = ?,
   306Ôćĺ					updated_at = ?
   307Ôćĺ				WHERE id = ?`,
   308Ôćĺ				name, name,
   309Ôćĺ				domain, domain,
   310Ôćĺ				company, company,
   311Ôćĺ				title, title,
   312Ôćĺ				phone, phone,
   313Ôćĺ				linkedin, linkedin,
   314Ôćĺ				string(InputCSV),
   315Ôćĺ				time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
   316Ôćĺ				time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
   317Ôćĺ				existingContact.id,
   318Ôćĺ			)
   319Ôćĺ			if err != nil {
   320Ôćĺ				return nil, fmt.Errorf("updating contact %s: %w", email, err)
   321Ôćĺ			}
   322Ôćĺ			missing := missingContactFieldsFromValues(name, email, company, title)
   323Ôćĺ			result.Contacts = append(result.Contacts, ResolvedContact{
   324Ôćĺ				ID:            existingContact.id,
   325Ôćĺ				Email:         email,
   326Ôćĺ				Name:          coalesce(name, existingContact.name),
   327Ôćĺ				Company:       coalesce(company, existingContact.company),
   328Ôćĺ				Title:         coalesce(title, existingContact.title),
   329Ôćĺ				Status:        "updated",
   330Ôćĺ				MissingFields: missing,
   331Ôćĺ				IsNew:         false,
   332Ôćĺ			})
   333Ôćĺ			updated++
   334Ôćĺ			continue
   335Ôćĺ		}
   336Ôćĺ
   337Ôćĺ		// Create new
   338Ôćĺ		id := newContactID()
   339Ôćĺ		now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
   340Ôćĺ		_, err = d.Exec(`
   341Ôćĺ			INSERT INTO contacts (id, source, email, name, domain, company, title, phone, linkedin_url, resolve_source, last_activity_at, updated_at)
   342Ôćĺ			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   343Ôćĺ			id, "resolve", email, name, domain, company, title, phone, linkedin, string(InputCSV), now, now,
   344Ôćĺ		)
   345Ôćĺ		if err != nil {
   346Ôćĺ			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating contact %s: %w", email, err)
   347Ôćĺ		}
   348Ôćĺ		missing := missingContactFieldsFromValues(name, email, company, title)
   349Ôćĺ		result.Contacts = append(result.Contacts, ResolvedContact{
   350Ôćĺ			ID:            id,
   351Ôćĺ			Email:         email,
   352Ôćĺ			Name:          name,
   353Ôćĺ			Company:       company,
   354Ôćĺ			Title:         title,
   355Ôćĺ			Status:        "created",
   356Ôćĺ			MissingFields: missing,
   357Ôćĺ			IsNew:         true,
   358Ôćĺ		})
   359Ôćĺ		created++
   360Ôćĺ	}
   361Ôćĺ
   362Ôćĺ	parts := []string{}
   363Ôćĺ	if created > 0 {
   364Ôćĺ		parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%d created", created))
   365Ôćĺ	}
   366Ôćĺ	if updated > 0 {
   367Ôćĺ		parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%d updated", updated))
   368Ôćĺ	}
   369Ôćĺ	if len(result.Skipped) > 0 {
   370Ôćĺ		parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%d skipped", len(result.Skipped)))
   371Ôćĺ	}
   372Ôćĺ	result.Summary = fmt.Sprintf("CSV import: %s", strings.Join(parts, ", "))
   373Ôćĺ	if created+updated == 0 {
   374Ôćĺ		result.Summary = "CSV import: no contacts resolved"
   375Ôćĺ	}
   376Ôćĺ
   377Ôćĺ	// Determine next step
   378Ôćĺ	hasEnrichNeeded := false
   379Ôćĺ	for _, c := range result.Contacts {
   380Ôćĺ		if len(c.MissingFields) > 0 {
   381Ôćĺ			hasEnrichNeeded = true
   382Ôćĺ			break
   383Ôćĺ		}
   384Ôćĺ	}
   385Ôćĺ	if hasEnrichNeeded {
   386Ôćĺ		result.Next = "enrich"
   387Ôćĺ	} else {
   388Ôćĺ		result.Next = "ready"
   389Ôćĺ	}
   390Ôćĺ
   391Ôćĺ	return result, nil
   392Ôćĺ}

   394Ôćĺ// --- LinkedIn Person ---
   395Ôćĺ
   396Ôćĺfunc resolveLinkedInPerson(d *sql.DB, input string) (*ResolveResult, error) {
   397Ôćĺ	slug := extractLinkedInSlug(input, "in")
   398Ôćĺ	if slug == "" {
   399Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not extract LinkedIn profile slug from: %s", input)
   400Ôćĺ	}
   401Ôćĺ
   402Ôćĺ	linkedinURL := normalizeLinkedInURL(input, "in")
   403Ôćĺ
   404Ôćĺ	// Check if contact with this LinkedIn URL already exists
   405Ôćĺ	existingContact, err := contactByLinkedIn(d, linkedinURL)
   406Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   407Ôćĺ		return nil, err
   408Ôćĺ	}
   409Ôćĺ
   410Ôćĺ	if existingContact != nil {
   411Ôćĺ		missing := missingContactFields(existingContact)
   412Ôćĺ		next := "ready"
   413Ôćĺ		if len(missing) > 0 {
   414Ôćĺ			next = "enrich"
   415Ôćĺ		}
   416Ôćĺ		return &ResolveResult{
   417Ôćĺ			InputType: InputLinkedInPerson,
   418Ôćĺ			Contacts: []ResolvedContact{{
   419Ôćĺ				ID:            existingContact.id,
   420Ôćĺ				Email:         existingContact.email,
   421Ôćĺ				Name:          existingContact.name,
   422Ôćĺ				Company:       existingContact.company,
   423Ôćĺ				Title:         existingContact.title,
   424Ôćĺ				Status:        "existing",
   425Ôćĺ				MissingFields: missing,
   426Ôćĺ				IsNew:         false,
   427Ôćĺ			}},
   428Ôćĺ			Summary: fmt.Sprintf("Found existing contact with LinkedIn: %s", slug),
   429Ôćĺ			Next:    next,
   430Ôćĺ		}, nil
   431Ôćĺ	}
   432Ôćĺ
   433Ôćĺ	// Create new contact with LinkedIn URL, most fields empty
   434Ôćĺ	id := newContactID()
   435Ôćĺ	name := slugToName(slug)
   436Ôćĺ	now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
   437Ôćĺ
   438Ôćĺ	_, err = d.Exec(`
   439Ôćĺ		INSERT INTO contacts (id, source, email, name, linkedin_url, resolve_source, last_activity_at, updated_at)
   440Ôćĺ		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   441Ôćĺ		id, "resolve", "", name, linkedinURL, string(InputLinkedInPerson), now, now,
   442Ôćĺ	)
   443Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   444Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating contact from LinkedIn: %w", err)
   445Ôćĺ	}
   446Ôćĺ
   447Ôćĺ	return &ResolveResult{
   448Ôćĺ		InputType: InputLinkedInPerson,
   449Ôćĺ		Contacts: []ResolvedContact{{
   450Ôćĺ			ID:            id,
   451Ôćĺ			Email:         "",
   452Ôćĺ			Name:          name,
   453Ôćĺ			Status:        "created",
   454Ôćĺ			MissingFields: []string{"email", "company", "title"},
   455Ôćĺ			IsNew:         true,
   456Ôćĺ		}},
   457Ôćĺ		Summary: fmt.Sprintf("Created contact from LinkedIn profile: %s (enrichment needed)", slug),
   458Ôćĺ		Next:    "enrich",
   459Ôćĺ	}, nil
   460Ôćĺ}

   462Ôćĺ// --- LinkedIn Company ---
   463Ôćĺ
   464Ôćĺfunc resolveLinkedInCompany(input string) (*ResolveResult, error) {
   465Ôćĺ	slug := extractLinkedInSlug(input, "company")
   466Ôćĺ	if slug == "" {
   467Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not extract LinkedIn company slug from: %s", input)
   468Ôćĺ	}
   469Ôćĺ
   470Ôćĺ	name := slugToName(slug)

   472Ôćĺ	return &ResolveResult{
   473Ôćĺ		InputType: InputLinkedInCompany,
   474Ôćĺ		Company: &ResolvedCompany{

   476Ôćĺ			Name:   name,
   477Ôćĺ		},
   478Ôćĺ		Summary: fmt.Sprintf("Identified company: %s (need to find people)", name),
   479Ôćĺ		Next:    "find_people",
   480Ôćĺ	}, nil
   481Ôćĺ}

   483Ôćĺ// --- Website ---
   484Ôćĺ
   485Ôćĺfunc resolveWebsite(input string) (*ResolveResult, error) {
   486Ôćĺ	domain := extractDomain(input)
   487Ôćĺ	if domain == "" {
   488Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not extract domain from: %s", input)
   489Ôćĺ	}
   490Ôćĺ
   491Ôćĺ	// Use the domain as a rough company name (capitalize first letter of each part)
   492Ôćĺ	name := domainToCompanyName(domain)
   493Ôćĺ
   494Ôćĺ	return &ResolveResult{
   495Ôćĺ		InputType: InputWebsite,
   496Ôćĺ		Company: &ResolvedCompany{
   497Ôćĺ			Domain: domain,
   498Ôćĺ			Name:   name,
   499Ôćĺ		},
   500Ôćĺ		Summary: fmt.Sprintf("Identified company at %s (need to find people)", domain),
   501Ôćĺ		Next:    "find_people",
   502Ôćĺ	}, nil
   503Ôćĺ}
   504Ôćĺ
   505Ôćĺ// --- Name + Company ---
   506Ôćĺ
   507Ôćĺfunc resolveNameCompany(d *sql.DB, name string, company string) (*ResolveResult, error) {
   508Ôćĺ	name = strings.TrimSpace(name)
   509Ôćĺ	company = strings.TrimSpace(company)
   510Ôćĺ
   511Ôćĺ	if name == "" {
   512Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("name is required for name+company resolve")
   513Ôćĺ	}
   514Ôćĺ
   515Ôćĺ	// Check if contact with this name+company already exists
   516Ôćĺ	existingContact, err := contactByNameCompany(d, name, company)
   517Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   518Ôćĺ		return nil, err
   519Ôćĺ	}
   520Ôćĺ
   521Ôćĺ	if existingContact != nil {
   522Ôćĺ		missing := missingContactFields(existingContact)
   523Ôćĺ		next := "ready"
   524Ôćĺ		if len(missing) > 0 {
   525Ôćĺ			next = "enrich"
   526Ôćĺ		}
   527Ôćĺ		return &ResolveResult{
   528Ôćĺ			InputType: InputNameCompany,
   529Ôćĺ			Contacts: []ResolvedContact{{
   530Ôćĺ				ID:            existingContact.id,
   531Ôćĺ				Email:         existingContact.email,
   532Ôćĺ				Name:          existingContact.name,
   533Ôćĺ				Company:       existingContact.company,
   534Ôćĺ				Title:         existingContact.title,
   535Ôćĺ				Status:        "existing",
   536Ôćĺ				MissingFields: missing,
   537Ôćĺ				IsNew:         false,
   538Ôćĺ			}},
   539Ôćĺ			Summary: fmt.Sprintf("Found existing contact: %s at %s", name, company),
   540Ôćĺ			Next:    next,
   541Ôćĺ		}, nil
   542Ôćĺ	}
   543Ôćĺ
   544Ôćĺ	// Create new contact with name + company, email is missing
   545Ôćĺ	id := newContactID()
   546Ôćĺ	now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
   547Ôćĺ
   548Ôćĺ	_, err = d.Exec(`
   549Ôćĺ		INSERT INTO contacts (id, source, email, name, company, resolve_source, last_activity_at, updated_at)
   550Ôćĺ		VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   551Ôćĺ		id, "resolve", "", name, company, string(InputNameCompany), now, now,
   552Ôćĺ	)
   553Ôćĺ	if err != nil {
   554Ôćĺ		return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating contact: %w", err)
   555Ôćĺ	}
   556Ôćĺ
   557Ôćĺ	missing := []string{"email", "title"}
   558Ôćĺ	if company == "" {
   559Ôćĺ		missing = append(missing, "company")
   560Ôćĺ	}
   561Ôćĺ
   562Ôćĺ	return &ResolveResult{
   563Ôćĺ		InputType: InputNameCompany,
   564Ôćĺ		Contacts: []ResolvedContact{{
   565Ôćĺ			ID:            id,
   566Ôćĺ			Email:         "",
   567Ôćĺ			Name:          name,
   568Ôćĺ			Company:       company,
   569Ôćĺ			Status:        "created",
   570Ôćĺ			MissingFields: missing,
   571Ôćĺ			IsNew:         true,
   572Ôćĺ		}},
   573Ôćĺ		Summary: fmt.Sprintf("Created contact %s (email and enrichment needed)", name),
   574Ôćĺ		Next:    "enrich",
   575Ôćĺ	}, nil
   576Ôćĺ}

   578Ôćĺ// --- Helpers ---
   579Ôćĺ
   580Ôćĺfunc newContactID() string {
   581Ôćĺ	return "contact_" + uuid.NewString()
   582Ôćĺ}
   583Ôćĺ
   584Ôćĺfunc looksLikeEmail(s string) bool {
   585Ôćĺ	_, err := mail.ParseAddress(s)
   586Ôćĺ	return err == nil
   587Ôćĺ}
   588Ôćĺ
   589Ôćĺfunc looksLikeURL(s string) bool {
   590Ôćĺ	s = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(s))
   591Ôćĺ	if strings.HasPrefix(s, "http://") || strings.HasPrefix(s, "https://") {
   592Ôćĺ		return true
   593Ôćĺ	}
   594Ôćĺ	// Check for domain-like patterns: has a dot, no spaces, no @
   595Ôćĺ	if strings.Contains(s, ".") && !strings.Contains(s, " ") && !strings.Contains(s, "@") {
   596Ôćĺ		return true
   597Ôćĺ	}
   598Ôćĺ	return false
   599Ôćĺ}
   600Ôćĺ
   601Ôćĺfunc domainFromEmail(email string) string {
   602Ôćĺ	parts := strings.SplitN(strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(email)), "@", 2)
   603Ôćĺ	if len(parts) != 2 {
   604Ôćĺ		return ""
   605Ôćĺ	}
   606Ôćĺ	return parts[1]
   607Ôćĺ}
   608Ôćĺ
   609Ôćĺfunc extractDomain(input string) string {
   610Ôćĺ	s := strings.TrimSpace(input)
   611Ôćĺ	s = strings.TrimPrefix(s, "https://")
   612Ôćĺ	s = strings.TrimPrefix(s, "http://")
   613Ôćĺ	s = strings.TrimPrefix(s, "www.")
   614Ôćĺ	// Take everything before the first /
   615Ôćĺ	if idx := strings.Index(s, "/"); idx >= 0 {
   616Ôćĺ		s = s[:idx]
   617Ôćĺ	}
   618Ôćĺ	if !strings.Contains(s, ".") {
   619Ôćĺ		return ""
   620Ôćĺ	}
   621Ôćĺ	return strings.ToLower(s)
   622Ôćĺ}
   623Ôćĺ
   624Ôćĺfunc extractLinkedInSlug(input string, kind string) string {
   625Ôćĺ	lower := strings.ToLower(input)
   626Ôćĺ	prefix := "linkedin.com/" + kind + "/"
   627Ôćĺ	idx := strings.Index(lower, prefix)
   628Ôćĺ	if idx < 0 {
   629Ôćĺ		return ""
   630Ôćĺ	}
   631Ôćĺ	rest := input[idx+len(prefix):]
   632Ôćĺ	// Remove trailing slash and query params
   633Ôćĺ	if slashIdx := strings.IndexAny(rest, "/?#"); slashIdx >= 0 {
   634Ôćĺ		rest = rest[:slashIdx]
   635Ôćĺ	}
   636Ôćĺ	return strings.TrimSpace(rest)
   637Ôćĺ}
   638Ôćĺ
   639Ôćĺfunc normalizeLinkedInURL(input string, kind string) string {
   640Ôćĺ	slug := extractLinkedInSlug(input, kind)
   641Ôćĺ	if slug == "" {
   642Ôćĺ		return ""
   643Ôćĺ	}
   644Ôćĺ	return "https://www.linkedin.com/" + kind + "/" + slug
   645Ôćĺ}
   646Ôćĺ
   647Ôćĺfunc slugToName(slug string) string {
   648Ôćĺ	// Convert "john-doe-123abc" to "John Doe"
   649Ôćĺ	// Remove trailing numeric/hash segments
   650Ôćĺ	parts := strings.Split(slug, "-")
   651Ôćĺ	var nameParts []string
   652Ôćĺ	for _, p := range parts {
   653Ôćĺ		// Skip if it looks like a LinkedIn hash suffix (all hex/digits)
   654Ôćĺ		if isHexOrDigits(p) && len(p) >= 5 {
   655Ôćĺ			continue
   656Ôćĺ		}
   657Ôćĺ		if p != "" {
   658Ôćĺ			nameParts = append(nameParts, capitalize(p))
   659Ôćĺ		}
   660Ôćĺ	}
   661Ôćĺ	return strings.Join(nameParts, " ")
   662Ôćĺ}
   663Ôćĺ
   664Ôćĺfunc isHexOrDigits(s string) bool {
   665Ôćĺ	for _, r := range s {
   666Ôćĺ		if !((r >= '0' && r <= '9') || (r >= 'a' && r <= 'f') || (r >= 'A' && r <= 'F')) {
   667Ôćĺ			return false
   668Ôćĺ		}
   669Ôćĺ	}
   670Ôćĺ	return len(s) > 0
   671Ôćĺ}
   672Ôćĺ
   673Ôćĺfunc capitalize(s string) string {
   674Ôćĺ	if s == "" {
   675Ôćĺ		return s
   676Ôćĺ	}
   677Ôćĺ	return strings.ToUpper(s[:1]) + s[1:]
   678Ôćĺ}
   679Ôćĺ
   680Ôćĺfunc domainToCompanyName(domain string) string {
   681Ôćĺ	// "acme.com" -> "Acme", "data-stack.io" -> "Data Stack"
   682Ôćĺ	parts := strings.Split(domain, ".")
   683Ôćĺ	if len(parts) < 2 {
   684Ôćĺ		return capitalize(domain)
   685Ôćĺ	}
   686Ôćĺ	// Use everything except the TLD
   687Ôćĺ	name := strings.Join(parts[:len(parts)-1], ".")
   688Ôćĺ	name = strings.ReplaceAll(name, "-", " ")
   689Ôćĺ	name = strings.ReplaceAll(name, "_", " ")
   690Ôćĺ	words := strings.Fields(name)
   691Ôćĺ	for i, w := range words {
   692Ôćĺ		words[i] = capitalize(w)
   693Ôćĺ	}
   694Ôćĺ	return strings.Join(words, " ")
   695Ôćĺ}

   707Ôćĺfunc contactByEmail(d *sql.DB, email string) (*contactRow, error) {

   709Ôćĺ	err := d.QueryRow(`
   710Ôćĺ		SELECT id, email, COALESCE(name,''), COALESCE(company,''), COALESCE(title,''), COALESCE(phone,'')
   711Ôćĺ		FROM contacts

   779Ôćĺfunc isSuppressed(d *sql.DB, email, domain string) (bool, error) {

   801Ôćĺ// buildColumnMap maps canonical field names to column indices by checking header aliases.
   802Ôćĺfunc buildColumnMap(header []string) map[string]int {
   803Ôćĺ	colMap := map[string]int{}
   804Ôćĺ
   805Ôćĺ	// Normalize headers
   806Ôćĺ	normalized := make([]string, len(header))
   807Ôćĺ	for i, h := range header {
   808Ôćĺ		normalized[i] = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(h))
   809Ôćĺ	}
   810Ôćĺ
   811Ôćĺ	// Check standard aliases
   812Ôćĺ	for field, aliases := range headerAliases {
   813Ôćĺ		for _, alias := range aliases {
   814Ôćĺ			for i, h := range normalized {
   815Ôćĺ				if h == alias {
   816Ôćĺ					colMap[field] = i
   817Ôćĺ					break
   818Ôćĺ				}
   819Ôćĺ			}
   820Ôćĺ			if _, ok := colMap[field]; ok {
   821Ôćĺ				break
   822Ôćĺ			}
   823Ôćĺ		}
   824Ôćĺ	}
   825Ôćĺ
   826Ôćĺ	// Handle special case: first_name + last_name
   827Ôćĺ	for i, h := range normalized {
   828Ôćĺ		switch h {
   829Ôćĺ		case "first name", "first_name", "firstname":
   830Ôćĺ			colMap["first_name"] = i
   831Ôćĺ		case "last name", "last_name", "lastname":
   832Ôćĺ			colMap["last_name"] = i
   833Ôćĺ		}
   834Ôćĺ	}
   835Ôćĺ
   836Ôćĺ	return colMap
   837Ôćĺ}
   838Ôćĺ
   839Ôćĺfunc colVal(record []string, colMap map[string]int, field string) string {
   840Ôćĺ	idx, ok := colMap[field]
   841Ôćĺ	if !ok || idx < 0 || idx >= len(record) {
   842Ôćĺ		return ""
   843Ôćĺ	}
   844Ôćĺ	return record[idx]
   845Ôćĺ}

1Ôćĺpackage enrich
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ// DefaultWaterfall defines the preferred provider order for each enrichable field.
     4Ôćĺ// When enriching a field, providers are tried in order until one succeeds or
     5Ôćĺ// returns cached data.
     6Ôćĺ//
     7Ôćĺ// Previously referenced apify-linkedin-person, apify-linkedin-company, and
     8Ôćĺ// apollo-email, but those providers were dead code (API calls were stubbed,
     9Ôćĺ// never made real HTTP requests). Enrichment now uses Convex/Unipile via
    10Ôćĺ// `nurge enrich company` and `nurge enrich person` subcommands.
    11Ôćĺ//
    12Ôćĺ// User-defined providers from .nurge/providers/ can still populate this map
    13Ôćĺ// if the waterfall-based enrichment is reactivated in the future.
    14Ôćĺvar DefaultWaterfall = map[string][]string{}
    15Ôćĺ
    16Ôćĺ// AllEnrichableFields returns every field that the waterfall can fill.
    17Ôćĺfunc AllEnrichableFields() []string {
    18Ôćĺ	return []string{
    19Ôćĺ		"email", "name", "title", "company", "phone",
    20Ôćĺ		"industry", "company_size", "funding_stage",
    21Ôćĺ		"headline", "location", "website",
    22Ôćĺ	}
    23Ôćĺ}
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ// WaterfallForField returns the ordered list of provider names to try for a field.
    26Ôćĺ// If the field isn't in the default waterfall, returns nil.
    27Ôćĺfunc WaterfallForField(field string) []string {
    28Ôćĺ	providers, ok := DefaultWaterfall[field]
    29Ôćĺ	if !ok {
    30Ôćĺ		return nil
    31Ôćĺ	}
    32Ôćĺ	// Return a copy to prevent mutation.
    33Ôćĺ	out := make([]string, len(providers))
    34Ôćĺ	copy(out, providers)
    35Ôćĺ	return out
    36Ôćĺ}

   133Ôćĺ// renderThinSkill generates the SKILL.md content for a single skill definition.
   134Ôćĺfunc renderThinSkill(def skillDef) string {
   135Ôćĺ	// YAML frontmatter
   136Ôćĺ	out := fmt.Sprintf("---\nname: %s\ndescription: %s\n---\n\n", def.Name, def.Description)
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ	// Bang command for context assembly
   139Ôćĺ	assembleCmd := fmt.Sprintf("nurge context assemble --task %s", string(def.Task))
   140Ôćĺ	if def.HasProspect {
   141Ôćĺ		assembleCmd += " --prospect $1"
   142Ôćĺ	}
   143Ôćĺ	assembleCmd += " --format markdown"
   144Ôćĺ	out += fmt.Sprintf("!`%s`\n\n", assembleCmd)
   145Ôćĺ
   146Ôćĺ	// Instructions
   147Ôćĺ	out += "Follow the assembled instructions above.\n"
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ	// Task-specific CLI actions
   150Ôćĺ	if len(def.CLIActions) > 0 {
   151Ôćĺ		out += "\nAvailable CLI commands for this task:\n"
   152Ôćĺ		for _, cmd := range def.CLIActions {
   153Ôćĺ			out += fmt.Sprintf("- `%s`\n", cmd)
   154Ôćĺ		}
   155Ôćĺ	}
   156Ôćĺ
   157Ôćĺ	return out
   158Ôćĺ}
   159Ôćĺ
   160Ôćĺ// ThinSkillTemplates returns all 17 thin skill templates keyed by skill name.
   161Ôćĺfunc ThinSkillTemplates() map[string]string {
   162Ôćĺ	templates := make(map[string]string, len(allSkillDefs))
   163Ôćĺ	for _, def := range allSkillDefs {
   164Ôćĺ		templates[def.Name] = renderThinSkill(def)
   165Ôćĺ	}
   166Ôćĺ	return templates
   167Ôćĺ}
   168Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ// ThinSkillTemplate returns the thin template content for a given skill name.
   170Ôćĺ// The second return value is false if the skill name is not recognized.
   171Ôćĺfunc ThinSkillTemplate(name string) (string, bool) {
   172Ôćĺ	for _, def := range allSkillDefs {
   173Ôćĺ		if def.Name == name {
   174Ôćĺ			return renderThinSkill(def), true
   175Ôćĺ		}
   176Ôćĺ	}
   177Ôćĺ	return "", false
   178Ôćĺ}
   179Ôćĺ

    32Ôćĺ// SearchHackerNews queries the HN Algolia API for company mentions. No auth required.
